整套首页
unit_11 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_11

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 522 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 TC SINGLE
As a history, Bunker's book can seem __________, prone as it is to diverting asides such as an engaging disquisition on the political science of early modern Europe.As a history, Bunker's book can seem __________, prone as it is to diverting asides such as an engaging disquisition on the political science of early modern Europe.
答案：E　haphazard
engageengagingprone
engage: standard unit headword | engaging: standard unit headword | prone: standard unit headword
#2 SE
There is frequently a protracted time interval between the introduction of an innovative musical composition and its public acceptance: the concert-going public often spurns the __________ in favor of the familiar for a prolonged period.There is frequently a protracted time interval between the introduction of an innovative musical composition and its public acceptance: the concert-going public often spurns the __________ in favor of the familiar for a prolonged period.
答案：C, D　novel; original
originalprolongprotracted
original: standard unit headword | prolong: standard unit headword | protracted: standard unit headword
#3 TC TRIPLE
A certain amount of theoretical frenzies about comics today is (i)__________. After all, similar frenzies have been seen in other art forms in the process of their rapid development, for instance, the debating about the paintings that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)__________ rarely preceded creative glory. On the contrary, it indicates that an artistic (iii)__________, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasingly strained emulation and diminishing returns is set in.A certain amount of theoretical frenzies about comics today is (i)__________. After all, similar frenzies have been seen in other art forms in the process of their rapid development, for instance, the debating about the paintings that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)__________ rarely preceded creative glory. On the contrary, it indicates that an artistic (iii)__________, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasingly strained emulation and diminishing returns is set in.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　understandable; arousal; breakthrough
renaissanceperplexprecede
renaissance: standard unit headword | perplex: standard unit headword | precede: standard unit headword
#4 TC TRIPLE
Compared with, say, the precision of a skein of geese winging its way across the autumn sky in a V formation, the seasonal marches of grazers across the Serengeti seem (i)__________, and at times even (ii)__________. But, in fact, years of careful observation by scientists have shown that there is (iii)__________ those migrations of zebras, wildebeests, and Thomson’s gazelles.Compared with, say, the precision of a skein of geese winging its way across the autumn sky in a V formation, the seasonal marches of grazers across the Serengeti seem (i)__________, and at times even (ii)__________. But, in fact, years of careful observation by scientists have shown that there is (iii)__________ those migrations of zebras, wildebeests, and Thomson’s gazelles.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　disorganized; chaotic; a definite order to
observationgrandeurpurposeful
observation: standard unit headword | grandeur: standard unit headword | purposeful: standard unit headword
#5 TC DOUBLE
Interest in creating handheld computers is fueled by the desire to shrink the size of the electronic circuitry and to create exceptionally small mechanical systems. At this scale, however, physical (i)__________ poses unique challenges. Machining, positioning, and assembling parts by hand are easy at microscopic scales but at minute scales they are far from (ii)__________.Interest in creating handheld computers is fueled by the desire to shrink the size of the electronic circuitry and to create exceptionally small mechanical systems. At this scale, however, physical (i)__________ poses unique challenges. Machining, positioning, and assembling parts by hand are easy at microscopic scales but at minute scales they are far from (ii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　manipulation; routine
circuitryinterest
circuitry: standard unit headword | interest: standard unit headword
#6 TC TRIPLE
For a time in the early Middle Ages, Latin culture came close to (i)__________: the witness to that is the (ii)__________ of manuscript copies of texts datable to the period. The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)__________, a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to us forever.For a time in the early Middle Ages, Latin culture came close to (i)__________: the witness to that is the (ii)__________ of manuscript copies of texts datable to the period. The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)__________, a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to us forever.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　vanishing; paucity; virtually came to a standstill
paucityvirtuallyproliferation
paucity: standard unit headword | virtually: standard unit headword | proliferation: standard unit headword
#7 SE
Between the late 1800s and early 1900s, various institutional structures emerged that set researchers in scientific fields apart as a professional class and moderated disputes by __________ some kinds of knowledge as real science.Between the late 1800s and early 1900s, various institutional structures emerged that set researchers in scientific fields apart as a professional class and moderated disputes by __________ some kinds of knowledge as real science.
答案：B, F　sanctioning; endorsing
moderateemerge
moderate: standard unit headword | emerge: standard unit headword

In reviewing cases decided by lower courts, Supreme Court justices search for precedents to justify their arguments. Reliance on precedent (i)__________ judicial restraint: the precedent (ii)__________ a judge’s ability to determine the outcome of a case in a way that he or she might choose if there were no precedent.In reviewing cases decided by lower courts, Supreme Court justices search for precedents to justify their arguments. Reliance on precedent (i)__________ judicial restraint: the precedent (ii)__________ a judge’s ability to determine the outcome of a case in a way that he or she might choose if there were no precedent.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　promotes; constraints
judicialprecedent
judicial: standard unit headword | precedent: standard unit headword
#9 SE
His premiership, seemingly cast-iron a year ago, is now so vulnerable that even a good day at the office does no more than buy him a few weeks of __________ from rebels within his own party.His premiership, seemingly cast-iron a year ago, is now so vulnerable that even a good day at the office does no more than buy him a few weeks of __________ from rebels within his own party.
答案：C, D　respite; relief

答案：A　tempers
anecdotemalfeasance

In a competitive news market, producers can use slant to differentiate their products and stave off price competition.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　accuracy; bias
flawcultivate
flaw: standard unit headword | cultivate: standard unit headword

In his perceptive study of the emergence and evolution of modern urban planning in Paris, Nicholas Papayanis challenges the widespread view of Baron Georges-Eugene Haussmann as (i) __________ figure in modern conceptions of the city. Although Papayanis (ii) __________ the importance of Haussmann's accomplishments and public work projects in the creation of modern Paris, he nevertheless argues that the forms of city planning and approaches to urban space these works display are less (iii) __________ than has hitherto been thought.In his perceptive study of the emergence and evolution of modern urban planning in Paris, Nicholas Papayanis challenges the widespread view of Baron Georges-Eugene Haussmann as (i) __________ figure in modern conceptions of the city. Although Papayanis (ii) __________ the importance of Haussmann's accomplishments and public work projects in the creation of modern Paris, he nevertheless argues that the forms of city planning and approaches to urban space these works display are less (iii) __________ than has hitherto been thought.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　the seminal; concedes; original
originalemergencegainsay
original: standard unit headword | emergence: standard unit headword | gainsay: standard unit headword

Hsu is __________ by nature, so it is no surprise that the clothes she designs have consistently flouted established fashion trends.Hsu is __________ by nature, so it is no surprise that the clothes she designs have consistently flouted established fashion trends.
答案：A, C　independent; freewheeling

sociable: standard unit headword | finicky: standard unit headword

Although that combination of personality traits is not uncommon, it is rare for a book that is so transparently an exercise in (iii)__________ to have the exact opposite of its intended effect.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　self-serving; self-regard; self-aggrandizement
effectmemoir
effect: standard unit headword | memoir: standard unit headword

Throughout his career he has never displayed a disinterested respect for the truth--for what was actually said and done rather than for what the wishes had been. Blank 2
答案：A, E　cavalier disregard for; ironic

答案：C　inscrutable

答案：A　independently

Blank 2

答案：A, F　inflammatory; provocative

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　inverted; defend her findings; concluded

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　taxing; sanity
intrude
intrude: standard unit headword
#51 TC TRIPLE
The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　melodramatic; tendentious; psychological acuity
understate
understate: standard unit headword
#52 SE
Much of the literature about railroads seeks someone to __________, and it is thus replete with encomiums to railroad entrepreneurs and managers.Much of the literature about railroads seeks someone to __________, and it is thus replete with encomiums to railroad entrepreneurs and managers.
答案：B, E　exalt; valorize
valorize
valorize: standard unit headword
#53 TC SINGLE
The pupil had a reputation for obduracy, but the teacher found her to be, on the contrary, quite __________.The pupil had a reputation for obduracy, but the teacher found her to be, on the contrary, quite __________.
答案：C　tractable
astute
astute: standard unit headword
#54 TC DOUBLE
When the United States government created the Post Office at the founding of the republic, it didn't invite rival postal firms to compete; in fact, it created a monopoly. That monopoly, however, was (i) __________ free expression because of policies Congress adopted, which (ii) __________ the circulation of newspapers irrespective of their viewpoint and spread postal service throughout the country.When the United States government created the Post Office at the founding of the republic, it didn't invite rival postal firms to compete; in fact, it created a monopoly. That monopoly, however, was (i) __________ free expression because of policies Congress adopted, which (ii) __________ the circulation of newspapers irrespective of their viewpoint and spread postal service throughout the country.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　conducive to; subsidized
monopoly
monopoly: standard unit headword
#55 TC TRIPLE
Given our species' increasing numbers and appetites--which are reflected in and compounded by global climate change--even (i)__________ species are likely to become endangered within the foreseeable future. If we are to avoid a calamitous loss of biodiversity, according to Golbe, we cannot simply let nature take its course. Ongoing conservation management is (ii)__________, as we have, in a sense, become nature, and the responsibilities that come with the role cannot be (iii)__________.Given our species' increasing numbers and appetites--which are reflected in and compounded by global climate change--even (i)__________ species are likely to become endangered within the foreseeable future. If we are to avoid a calamitous loss of biodiversity, according to Golbe, we cannot simply let nature take its course. Ongoing conservation management is (ii)__________, as we have, in a sense, become nature, and the responsibilities that come with the role cannot be (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　now common; obligatory; jettisoned
ineffective
ineffective: standard unit headword

Early practitioners of the natural sciences developed methods to remove distortions caused by either the research environment or the researcher. Such methods, especially with respect to the researcher, were considered to (i)__________ those (ii)__________ subjectivity whose unbridled expression was thought to (iii)__________ research.Early practitioners of the natural sciences developed methods to remove distortions caused by either the research environment or the researcher. Such methods, especially with respect to the researcher, were considered to (i)__________ those (ii)__________ subjectivity whose unbridled expression was thought to (iii)__________ research.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　restrain; incursions of; corrupt
corrupt
corrupt: standard unit headword
#57 TC SINGLE
By deliberately dripping paint on their canvases, expressionists affirmed that paint is not __________ entity, something merely to be moved where the artist pleases, but rather a material possessing a fluid energy that the painter attempts to control.By deliberately dripping paint on their canvases, expressionists affirmed that paint is not __________ entity, something merely to be moved where the artist pleases, but rather a material possessing a fluid energy that the painter attempts to control.
答案：D　a passive
expressive
expressive: standard unit headword
#58 SE
The tribes’ __________ a settled mode of living was derived from their long-standing traditions, which, though differing from one tribal group to another, always included a resistance to nomadic lifestyles.The tribes’ __________ a settled mode of living was derived from their long-standing traditions, which, though differing from one tribal group to another, always included a resistance to nomadic lifestyles.
答案：B, C　proclivity toward; predilection for

nomadic: standard unit headword

Blank 2

答案：D, F　awkward; ungainly

Blank 2

#62 TC TRIPLE
Gravitational waves—ripples in the geometry of space-time—are analogous to electromagnetic waves. The challenge in trying to observe these waves directly is that they are extremely weak. To make waves large enough to be (i)__________, the most (ii)__________ events in the universe are required: supernova explosions, the formation of black holes, or the collision of stars. Even so, the effects are (iii)__________. The geometry changes so little that a distance of several kilometers changes by less than the diameter of proton.Gravitational waves—ripples in the geometry of space-time—are analogous to electromagnetic waves. The challenge in trying to observe these waves directly is that they are extremely weak. To make waves large enough to be (i)__________, the most (ii)__________ events in the universe are required: supernova explosions, the formation of black holes, or the collision of stars. Even so, the effects are (iii)__________. The geometry changes so little that a distance of several kilometers changes by less than the diameter of proton.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　detectable; violent; minuscule
effectminuscule

答案：C, D　purposefulness; design

Blank 2

Blank 2
Blank 3

It is true that windmills could be built virtually everywhere, whereas water mills (ii)__________. However, water mill’s great capacity and reliability provided a better (iii)__________ the money required to build the mill.

This news must have been (iii) __________ to those with a vested interest in the medical orthodoxy of the time.The advance of medical knowledge is not always (i) __________; on the contrary, it is often the result of (ii) __________. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　cumulative; heretical departures; jarring

答案：C, D　idiosyncratic; impugn
observationemerge
observation: standard unit headword | emerge: standard unit headword
#102 TC TRIPLE
Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)__________, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)__________, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)__________.Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)__________, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)__________, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　unavoidable; shy away; emerges victorious
emerge
emerge: standard unit headword
#103 SE
Flawed as it may be for it is constructed by subjective scientists, science itself has methods that help us __________ our bias and talk about objective reality with some validity.Flawed as it may be for it is constructed by subjective scientists, science itself has methods that help us __________ our bias and talk about objective reality with some validity.
答案：A, F　bypass; circumvent
flaw
flaw: standard unit headword
#104 TC SINGLE
The subjects who are engaged in multi-tasks often __________ deterioration in their performance over time, and therefore the need to concentrate can apparently enhance long-term efficiency.The subjects who are engaged in multi-tasks often __________ deterioration in their performance over time, and therefore the need to concentrate can apparently enhance long-term efficiency.
答案：D　displayed
engage
engage: standard unit headword
#105 TC TRIPLE
Penrose is (i) __________ theoretical physicists for engaging in (ii) __________ by (iii) __________ what is implied by the known data. One of his favorite examples is inflationary cosmology, which, according to him, is treated as an established theory despite a lack of evidence.Penrose is (i) __________ theoretical physicists for engaging in (ii) __________ by (iii) __________ what is implied by the known data. One of his favorite examples is inflationary cosmology, which, according to him, is treated as an established theory despite a lack of evidence.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　critical of; conjecture; venturing beyond
engageengaging
engage: standard unit headword | engaging: standard unit headword
#106 SE
Many innovative breakthroughs come about when people venture beyond their own areas of expertise, because often it takes an outsider to ask the naïve question that may yield __________ solution.Many innovative breakthroughs come about when people venture beyond their own areas of expertise, because often it takes an outsider to ask the naïve question that may yield __________ solution.
答案：B, F　an unconventional; an original
original
original: standard unit headword
#107 TC DOUBLE
Given the (i)__________ committees and the (ii)__________ nature of its investigation, it would be unreasonable to gainsay the committee’s conclusions at first glance.Given the (i)__________ committees and the (ii)__________ nature of its investigation, it would be unreasonable to gainsay the committee’s conclusions at first glance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　sterling reputation of; exhaustive
gainsay
gainsay: standard unit headword
#108 TC SINGLE
For the urban researcher, the long lives of ancient cities can provide ample chronological data, making up for the paucity stemming from relative __________ of most present-day cities.For the urban researcher, the long lives of ancient cities can provide ample chronological data, making up for the paucity stemming from relative __________ of most present-day cities.
答案：E　youthfulness
paucity
paucity: standard unit headword
#109 SE
Scientific discovery calls for a difficult balance: intrepid advocacy of new ideas must often be __________ by the results of self-imposed trials.Scientific discovery calls for a difficult balance: intrepid advocacy of new ideas must often be __________ by the results of self-imposed trials.
答案：A, B　tempered; moderated
moderate
moderate: standard unit headword
#110 TC TRIPLE
Both very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)__________ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the review’s task is more (ii)__________: the author’s useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)__________ the useless and tedious.Both very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)__________ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the review’s task is more (ii)__________: the author’s useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)__________ the useless and tedious.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　censure; demanding; sifted from

#111 TC SINGLE

答案：B　pervasive
proliferation
proliferation: standard unit headword
#112 TC DOUBLE
So (i)__________ is the reputation of the city's police force for (ii)__________ that whenever a new police chief takes office, he or she routinely promises to clean up the force.So (i)__________ is the reputation of the city's police force for (ii)__________ that whenever a new police chief takes office, he or she routinely promises to clean up the force.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　persistent; corruption

答案：C, E　minimal; trifling

#114 TC TRIPLE
Viewing people as “social atoms” that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.Viewing people as “social atoms” that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　law-like regularities; chaotic; self-perpetuating

答案：E　exculpation

It is not just the entry of new collections into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors’ interests are also becoming more (ii)__________. Those who once concentrate on the work of either the nineteenth century pioneers or the twentieth century modernists are now keen to have (iii)__________ collections.Now that photographic prints have become a popular field for collecting, auctions are becoming more (i)__________. It is not just the entry of new collections into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors’ interests are also becoming more (ii)__________. Those who once concentrate on the work of either the nineteenth century pioneers or the twentieth century modernists are now keen to have (iii)__________ collections.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　competitive; wide-ranging; comprehensive

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　precipitate; forbore

答案：C　fanciful

答案：C, F　restorative; tonic

The only (iii)__________ sensory deprivation of the sessions came from the handsome color slides favored by the corporate presenters.Despite the occasional (i)__________ of their venues, the culture of corporate conferences is a deeply (ii)__________ one, each day consisted of nearly nine hours of continuous lectures and panels enlivened by pleasantries or anything that could be construed as a joke. The only (iii)__________ sensory deprivation of the sessions came from the handsome color slides favored by the corporate presenters.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　opulence; ascetic; respite from

Diaries and letters are rarely sites for (ii)__________.It would be naïve to treat remarks made in diaries or personal letters as giving especially candid access to historical truth or even as being expressions of the writer’s true state of mind, since the (i)__________ for exaggeration and deception in those forms is virtually nonexistent. Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　penalty; balanced reflection

Yet when problems are (ii)__________, or require combining varied sources of information, computers are (iii)__________ human intelligence.

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　palliative; curative

答案：C　promise

In a number of instances, investors hoping to tap into the region’s meteoric growth have instead faced problems ranging from unpredictable management practices to outright __________.In a number of instances, investors hoping to tap into the region’s meteoric growth have instead faced problems ranging from unpredictable management practices to outright __________.
答案：A, C　malfeasance; fraudulence

This judgment cohered with the high level of mathematical theory, which (ii)__________ mathematical computation together with empirical observation as (iii)__________ of science and denied any role to speculative hypotheses of a strongly theoretical nature.The historian of ancient science Otto Neugebauer concluded that Babylonian astronomical texts are (i)__________ because everything has been eliminated from the astronomy except observations and the mathematical consequences of an initial hypothesis about the fundamental character of the astronomical movements. This judgment cohered with the high level of mathematical theory, which (ii)__________ mathematical computation together with empirical observation as (iii)__________ of science and denied any role to speculative hypotheses of a strongly theoretical nature.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　scientific; admitted; necessary characteristic

答案：B　obscure

Blank 2
答案：A, D　unethical; skullduggery

But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the established view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the established view.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine

Though acquaintances are first overwhelmed by his __________, they soon appreciate that, contrary to appearances, he is not without self-interest.Though acquaintances are first overwhelmed by his __________, they soon appreciate that, contrary to appearances, he is not without self-interest.
答案：B　magnanimity

Blank 2
答案：A, D　disinterested; exacting

And yet, despite these powerful (iii)__________ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)__________. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)__________ public trust in the way companies were run. Blank 2

答案：B, D, G　atypical; weakened; counterforce

Indeed, by the 1920s Washington had become such (ii)__________ personage that inevitably someone had to go to the other extreme and try to (iii)__________ the legend.With the emergence of scientific history-writing in the late nineteenth century, several authors sought to ignore the glowing myths surrounding George Washington and uncover the human being within, but their biographies were still (i)__________ enough that Washington remained a marbled and remote figure. Indeed, by the 1920s Washington had become such (ii)__________ personage that inevitably someone had to go to the other extreme and try to (iii)__________ the legend.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　laudatory; a deified; debunk

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　wistful; valedictory

答案：C, E　obscure; opaque

The first clarifying step must be to recognize that “democracy” itself can, in the abstract, (i)__________ us as we think of our society and our perception of democracy as citizens. The experience of living in a democracy (ii)__________ each generation. A feature of democratic is that something that benefits us in one generation may no longer be a benefit to the next. Thus, experiencing democracy in the twenty-first-century is (iii)__________ the political order our ancestors cherished in 1901.How does one evaluate the effects of modern democracy on individuals? The first clarifying step must be to recognize that “democracy” itself can, in the abstract, (i)__________ us as we think of our society and our perception of democracy as citizens. The experience of living in a democracy (ii)__________ each generation. A feature of democratic is that something that benefits us in one generation may no longer be a benefit to the next. Thus, experiencing democracy in the twenty-first-century is (iii)__________ the political order our ancestors cherished in 1901.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　mislead; changes for; radically different from

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　fallacious; adverse

答案：A, B　abound; proliferate
proliferate

Barry was a skilled __________: though his arguments were completely flawed, they were clever-sounding enough to deceive everyone who heard them.Barry was a skilled __________: though his arguments were completely flawed, they were clever-sounding enough to deceive everyone who heard them.
答案：A　sophist

flaw: standard unit headword
#158 TC TRIPLE
The research on otters' environmental requirements is surprisingly (i)__________. One reason for this has to do with the estimation of how much they use different areas. Doing so may be (ii)__________ in some kinds of terrain, such as Shetland where the Eurasian otters are active in daytime and have clear individual markings. There it is possible to identify the individuals over stretches of coast of a few kilometers and to see what kinds of coast they use. However, the field conditions are (iii)__________.The research on otters' environmental requirements is surprisingly (i)__________. One reason for this has to do with the estimation of how much they use different areas. Doing so may be (ii)__________ in some kinds of terrain, such as Shetland where the Eurasian otters are active in daytime and have clear individual markings. There it is possible to identify the individuals over stretches of coast of a few kilometers and to see what kinds of coast they use. However, the field conditions are (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　difficult; relatively simple; exceptional
terrain
terrain: standard unit headword
#159 TC DOUBLE
Journalist Michael Pollan is nothing if not (i) __________, committed to investigating our eating habits through (ii) __________ and unwilling to pass judgment on any food-related practice that he has not witnessed firsthand or even joined in.Journalist Michael Pollan is nothing if not (i) __________, committed to investigating our eating habits through (ii) __________ and unwilling to pass judgment on any food-related practice that he has not witnessed firsthand or even joined in.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　an empiricist; careful observation
observation
observation: standard unit headword
#160 TC SINGLE
Many legislators who helped Roosevelt shape the New Deal __________ the fact that emerging social problems affected every segment of the population; nonetheless, they often acted with a view to aiding only their own constituents.Many legislators who helped Roosevelt shape the New Deal __________ the fact that emerging social problems affected every segment of the population; nonetheless, they often acted with a view to aiding only their own constituents.
答案：B　bemoaned
emerge
emerge: standard unit headword
#161 SE
If giant x-ray flares churn circumstellar disks enough to keep newborn planets, such as Earth once was, from spiraling into their suns, it would be an ironic twist on our conception of x-ray flares as __________.If giant x-ray flares churn circumstellar disks enough to keep newborn planets, such as Earth once was, from spiraling into their suns, it would be an ironic twist on our conception of x-ray flares as __________.
答案：A, E　dangerous; perilous
ironic
ironic: standard unit headword

Early practitioners of the natural sciences developed methods to remove distortions caused by either the research environment or the researcher. Such methods, especially with respect to the researcher, were considered to (i)__________ those (ii)__________ subjectivity whose unbridled expression was thought to (iii)__________ research.Early practitioners of the natural sciences developed methods to remove distortions caused by either the research environment or the researcher. Such methods, especially with respect to the researcher, were considered to (i)__________ those (ii)__________ subjectivity whose unbridled expression was thought to (iii)__________ research.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　restrain; incursions of; corrupt

corrupt: standard unit headword

Given the (i) __________ the committees and the (ii) __________ nature of its investigation, it would be unreasonable to gainsay the committee's conclusions at first glance.Given the (i) __________ the committees and the (ii) __________ nature of its investigation, it would be unreasonable to gainsay the committee's conclusions at first glance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　sterling reputation of; exhaustive
gainsay
gainsay: standard unit headword

His __________ speaking style notwithstanding, William Perkins has long been seen as the moderate face of his political party.His __________ speaking style notwithstanding, William Perkins has long been seen as the moderate face of his political party.
答案：E　vehement
moderate
moderate: standard unit headword
#165 SE
To call Kermode the finest English critic of his generation would be a __________ compliment, since not many of its population are professionally engaged in literary criticism.To call Kermode the finest English critic of his generation would be a __________ compliment, since not many of its population are professionally engaged in literary criticism.
答案：C, E　paltry; meager
engage
engage: standard unit headword
#166 TC TRIPLE
Despite the occasional (i)__________ of their venues, the culture of corporate conferences is a deeply (ii)__________ conference, each day consisted of nearly nine hours of continuous lectures and panels enlivened by pleasantries or anything that could be construed as a joke. The only (iii)__________ sensory deprivation of the sessions came from the handsome color slides favored by the corporate presenters.Despite the occasional (i)__________ of their venues, the culture of corporate conferences is a deeply (ii)__________ conference, each day consisted of nearly nine hours of continuous lectures and panels enlivened by pleasantries or anything that could be construed as a joke. The only (iii)__________ sensory deprivation of the sessions came from the handsome color slides favored by the corporate presenters.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　opulence; ascetic; respite from
respite
respite: standard unit headword
#167 TC DOUBLE
Except for a few passages in the biography in which the subject's flaws are (i)__________, the author's treatment of her subject is (ii)__________.Except for a few passages in the biography in which the subject's flaws are (i)__________, the author's treatment of her subject is (ii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　too harshly judged; judicious
flaw
flaw: standard unit headword
#168 TC SINGLE
While many books have emphasized the differences between Delaney and his father - and his need to differentiate himself from his father - Delaney takes pains in this memoir to __________ his closeness with his dad.While many books have emphasized the differences between Delaney and his father - and his need to differentiate himself from his father - Delaney takes pains in this memoir to __________ his closeness with his dad.
答案：A　underscore
memoir
memoir: standard unit headword
#169 SE
The spacecraft’s considerable heft forces an unusually __________ route that meanders through the solar system and depends on the gravitational pull of three heavenly bodies.The spacecraft’s considerable heft forces an unusually __________ route that meanders through the solar system and depends on the gravitational pull of three heavenly bodies.
答案：B, C　circuitous; indirect
circuitous
circuitous: standard unit headword
#170 TC TRIPLE
When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the establish view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the establish view.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine
emerge
emerge: standard unit headword
#171 TC DOUBLE
At Cerro Portezuelo, the task of separating grinding tools from the larger collection of excavated stone objects was (i) __________ the ancient practice of recycling grinding tool fragments for building materials, hammer stones, and other purposes that (ii) __________ their original use.At Cerro Portezuelo, the task of separating grinding tools from the larger collection of excavated stone objects was (i) __________ the ancient practice of recycling grinding tool fragments for building materials, hammer stones, and other purposes that (ii) __________ their original use.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　complicated by; obscured
original
original: standard unit headword
#172 SE
Although the essayist’s arguments did not __________ her most perceptive readers, the extreme subtlety of the points she made explains why she was misinterpreted by most critics of her day.Although the essayist’s arguments did not __________ her most perceptive readers, the extreme subtlety of the points she made explains why she was misinterpreted by most critics of her day.
答案：B, E　confound; perplex

perplex: standard unit headword
#173 TC SINGLE
The senator's attempt to convince the public that he was not interested in running for a second term was as __________ as his opponent's attempt to disguise her intention to run against him.The senator's attempt to convince the public that he was not interested in running for a second term was as __________ as his opponent's attempt to disguise her intention to run against him.
答案：C　disingenuous
interest
interest: standard unit headword
#174 TC TRIPLE
Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)__________, communal devil feeding is (ii)__________ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)__________ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)__________, communal devil feeding is (ii)__________ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)__________ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　free-for-all; structured; ritualized
purposeful
purposeful: standard unit headword
#175 TC DOUBLE
The building affairs minister rightly recognizes that the current planning system--under which the government controls every aspect of construction--creates disastrous developments, but she is wrong to propose the opposite: the wholesale (i) __________ of the building market. Such a complete (ii) __________ of responsibility on the part of the state can hardly be in the public's interest.The building affairs minister rightly recognizes that the current planning system--under which the government controls every aspect of construction--creates disastrous developments, but she is wrong to propose the opposite: the wholesale (i) __________ of the building market. Such a complete (ii) __________ of responsibility on the part of the state can hardly be in the public's interest.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　liberalization; abnegation

interest: standard unit headword

答案：D, F　paucity; dearth

答案：C　introspection

memoir: standard unit headword

Blank 1

答案：B, E　corruption; integrity

The fact that all major planets have satellites was __________ before the space age; however, the numbers of known satellites for these planets have been greatly expanded by spacecraft observations.The fact that all major planets have satellites was __________ before the space age; however, the numbers of known satellites for these planets have been greatly expanded by spacecraft observations.
答案：B　recognized

#181 SE
Even though the original settlement may not hold up, it at least proves that the deadlock can be broken and that a hitherto __________ party is ready to bargain.Even though the original settlement may not hold up, it at least proves that the deadlock can be broken and that a hitherto __________ party is ready to bargain.
答案：A, B　implacable; unyielding

The (i)__________ nature of the candidate's comments is calculated. As a long-standing target of critics who regard him as radical, he understands that he needs to be as (ii)__________ as possible if he is to overcome those critiques and appear as a (iii)__________ leader.The (i)__________ nature of the candidate's comments is calculated. As a long-standing target of critics who regard him as radical, he understands that he needs to be as (ii)__________ as possible if he is to overcome those critiques and appear as a (iii)__________ leader.

Blank 2

Diaries and letters are rarely sites for (ii) __________.It would be naive to treat remarks made in diaries or personal letters as giving especially candid access to historical truth or even as being expressions of the writer's true state of mind, since (i) __________ for exaggeration and deception in those forms is virtually nonexistent. Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　penalty; balanced reflection
virtually

答案：E　germane to

#187 TC DOUBLE
The controversy about Alexander the Great's personality derives from the fact that our sources are (i) __________, all eyewitness accounts having perished. What remains is, at best, (ii) __________ (one history, for instance, is based largely on the now-lost memoirs of Alexander's alleged half-brother, Ptolemy) and at worst, highly unreliable.The controversy about Alexander the Great's personality derives from the fact that our sources are (i) __________, all eyewitness accounts having perished. What remains is, at best, (ii) __________ (one history, for instance, is based largely on the now-lost memoirs of Alexander's alleged half-brother, Ptolemy) and at worst, highly unreliable.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　inadequate; secondhand

memoir: standard unit headword
#188 TC SINGLE
As a genre that prizes authenticity over artifice, memoir puts a special pressure on literary style: if the memoirist's language tends toward the highly lyrical, it can __________ the memoirist's claim to truth.As a genre that prizes authenticity over artifice, memoir puts a special pressure on literary style: if the memoirist's language tends toward the highly lyrical, it can __________ the memoirist's claim to truth.
答案：C　hinder
memoir
memoir: standard unit headword
#189 SE
It is hardly __________ for today's film to try to blur the boundaries between the moral and the immoral: Hollywood has been doing that since at least the 1960s.It is hardly __________ for today's film to try to blur the boundaries between the moral and the immoral: Hollywood has been doing that since at least the 1960s.
答案：B, C　original; novel
original
original: standard unit headword
#190 TC TRIPLE
Now that photographic prints have become a popular field for collecting, auctions are becoming more (i) __________. It is not just the entry of new collectors into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors' interests are also becoming more (ii) __________. Those who once concentrated on the work of either the nineteenth-century pioneers or the twentieth-century modernists are now keen to have (iii) __________ collections.Now that photographic prints have become a popular field for collecting, auctions are becoming more (i) __________. It is not just the entry of new collectors into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors' interests are also becoming more (ii) __________. Those who once concentrated on the work of either the nineteenth-century pioneers or the twentieth-century modernists are now keen to have (iii) __________ collections.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　competitive; wide-ranging; comprehensive

interest: standard unit headword
#191 TC DOUBLE
Nordhaus predicts that in the future we will increasingly be (i) __________ ecological problems like global warming rather than (ii) __________ them. We may, for example, make some headway in limiting emissions that contribute to warming, but much of our work will be in adapting to ecological problems and alleviating their effects.Nordhaus predicts that in the future we will increasingly be (i) __________ ecological problems like global warming rather than (ii) __________ them. We may, for example, make some headway in limiting emissions that contribute to warming, but much of our work will be in adapting to ecological problems and alleviating their effects.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　managing; solving

答案：B, C　original; novel

original: standard unit headword

答案：E　a protean
prone
prone: standard unit headword

Both very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)__________ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the reviewer's task is more (ii)__________: the author's useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)__________ the useless and tedious.Both very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)__________ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the reviewer's task is more (ii)__________: the author's useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)__________ the useless and tedious.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　censure; demanding; sifted from

Argument may be an overly (i) __________ word to apply to the gossamer contrivance that is A Summer of Hummingbirds. In what seems a self-conscious (ii) __________ of its mascot the book flits from one subject or moment in history to another, following the various whims of its author.Argument may be an overly (i) __________ word to apply to the gossamer contrivance that is A Summer of Hummingbirds. In what seems a self-conscious (ii) __________ of its mascot the book flits from one subject or moment in history to another, following the various whims of its author.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　robust; emulation
mascot

They applaud the musicals of the 1930s and 1940s, whose plethora of stars, jokes, dances, witty dialogue, and general gaiety make today’s offerings seem __________ by comparison.They applaud the musicals of the 1930s and 1940s, whose plethora of stars, jokes, dances, witty dialogue, and general gaiety make today’s offerings seem __________ by comparison.
答案：A, E　cheerless; gloomy

答案：A　an irenic
effect
effect: standard unit headword

The experimental theater company's members know that their performances (i)__________ an audience, that they were dense and unpredictable and not always easy to digest. But none of the techniques used would be (ii)__________ anyone with an interest in music or films. Indeed, they would seem strange only to people who expected to see traditionally crafted plays. The actors therefore felt that theater critics' derisive commentary showed only that the critics (iii)__________ the company's work.The experimental theater company's members know that their performances (i)__________ an audience, that they were dense and unpredictable and not always easy to digest. But none of the techniques used would be (ii)__________ anyone with an interest in music or films. Indeed, they would seem strange only to people who expected to see traditionally crafted plays. The actors therefore felt that theater critics' derisive commentary showed only that the critics (iii)__________ the company's work.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　made demands on; alien to; misunderstood
interest
interest: standard unit headword
#199 TC DOUBLE
While writers on Parker often present her as an avaricious stick figure, thus making her an object of (i) __________, Nash, by contrast, gives a more (ii) __________ and sympathetic portrait of the woman.While writers on Parker often present her as an avaricious stick figure, thus making her an object of (i) __________, Nash, by contrast, gives a more (ii) __________ and sympathetic portrait of the woman.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　opprobrium; nuanced
nuance
nuance: standard unit headword
#200 TC SINGLE
Whether the director's action was pusillanimous or merely __________, the effect was the same: many board members argued against continuing to entrust leadership to a person who was perceived as unwilling to take risks.Whether the director's action was pusillanimous or merely __________, the effect was the same: many board members argued against continuing to entrust leadership to a person who was perceived as unwilling to take risks.
答案：C　prudent
effect
effect: standard unit headword
#201 SE
While normal floods resulting from usual monsoon rainfall are __________ the growth of crops, recently there has been an increase in the frequency of high-intensity floods that do not have such welcome effects.While normal floods resulting from usual monsoon rainfall are __________ the growth of crops, recently there has been an increase in the frequency of high-intensity floods that do not have such welcome effects.
答案：E, F　essential for; indispensable to

effect: standard unit headword
#202 TC TRIPLE
At a recent conference on ancient DNA, one presentation opened with the claim that the field was now mature and could move ahead with confidence. This (i)__________ is (ii)__________, as demonstrated at the conference by the many presentations that notably lacked an adequate methodology for ensuring that DNA was uncontaminated by material that is more recent. In fact, ancient DNA research presents extreme technical difficulties, in part precisely because of the (iii)__________ of surviving DNA.At a recent conference on ancient DNA, one presentation opened with the claim that the field was now mature and could move ahead with confidence. This (i)__________ is (ii)__________, as demonstrated at the conference by the many presentations that notably lacked an adequate methodology for ensuring that DNA was uncontaminated by material that is more recent. In fact, ancient DNA research presents extreme technical difficulties, in part precisely because of the (iii)__________ of surviving DNA.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　optimism; unfounded; corrupted nature
corrupt
corrupt: standard unit headword
#203 TC DOUBLE
The claim that large budget deficits significantly depress private investment and thereby hurt future wages and living standards is (i) __________; in reality, the (ii) __________ effects of budget deficits are tiny.The claim that large budget deficits significantly depress private investment and thereby hurt future wages and living standards is (i) __________; in reality, the (ii) __________ effects of budget deficits are tiny.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　fallacious; adverse
effect
effect: standard unit headword

When it comes to cheating in competitive sports and games, chess is a particular case that might seem all but __________, since after all the board and its pieces are out in the open for all to see.When it comes to cheating in competitive sports and games, chess is a particular case that might seem all but __________, since after all the board and its pieces are out in the open for all to see.
答案：A　invulnerable
all but
all but: standard unit headword

答案：A, E　A trepidation; E apprehension

Yet when problems are (ii)__________, or require combining varied sources, computers are (iii)__________ human intelligence.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　a well-defined; nuanced; no match for

The small errors of fact in the work are not sufficient to allow one to (i) __________ Johnson's main thesis, but their cumulative effect is to make the reader (ii) __________ of the quality of his research.The small errors of fact in the work are not sufficient to allow one to (i) __________ Johnson's main thesis, but their cumulative effect is to make the reader (ii) __________ of the quality of his research.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　dismiss; skeptical

#208 TC SINGLE
The processes by which people discover meaning in their lives are ultimately __________, in the sense that one's place in the larger social order emerges only through the narratives that people present to themselves and to others.The processes by which people discover meaning in their lives are ultimately __________, in the sense that one's place in the larger social order emerges only through the narratives that people present to themselves and to others.
答案：A　discursive

答案：E, F　lugubrious; gloomy

#210 TC TRIPLE
The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)__________. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)__________ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)__________ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)__________. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)__________ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)__________ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　atypical; weakened; counterforces
precedent
precedent: standard unit headword
#211 TC DOUBLE
From time to time, all scientific disciplines encounter observations that do not fit the theories of the day. At first such observations tend to be treated as (i) __________, but those scientists (ii) __________ them sometimes discover that they have to abandon established principles in order to account for the new findings.From time to time, all scientific disciplines encounter observations that do not fit the theories of the day. At first such observations tend to be treated as (i) __________, but those scientists (ii) __________ them sometimes discover that they have to abandon established principles in order to account for the new findings.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　anomalies; willing to investigate
observation
observation: standard unit headword
#212 TC TRIPLE
The quiet, careful member of a collaborative, creative pair may naturally be (i) __________ by posterity in comparison with the brash, dramatic one. Braque and Picasso created Cubism together, but the mercurial Picasso is regarded as the star of the movement while Braque, because he was so much more (ii) __________, has been relatively (iii) __________ by history.The quiet, careful member of a collaborative, creative pair may naturally be (i) __________ by posterity in comparison with the brash, dramatic one. Braque and Picasso created Cubism together, but the mercurial Picasso is regarded as the star of the movement while Braque, because he was so much more (ii) __________, has been relatively (iii) __________ by history.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　diminished; understated; neglected
understate
understate: standard unit headword
#213 TC SINGLE
Readers are interested in Marx's life because of his ideas, but Sperber's book downplays his ideas to make room for his life, illustrating why intellectual biography can sometimes be a curiously __________ genre.Readers are interested in Marx's life because of his ideas, but Sperber's book downplays his ideas to make room for his life, illustrating why intellectual biography can sometimes be a curiously __________ genre.
答案：C　self-defeating
interest
interest: standard unit headword
#214 SE
The nation's robust economic performance could be __________ by the persistent flaws in its economic institutions and structure that are the result of half-finished and misguided government policies.The nation's robust economic performance could be __________ by the persistent flaws in its economic institutions and structure that are the result of half-finished and misguided government policies.
答案：C, D　undermined; impaired
flaw
flaw: standard unit headword
#215 TC DOUBLE
There has been (i) __________ elephant's fabled mental capacities until recently, when these behavioral observations have begun to be (ii) __________ by brain science. MRI scans of an elephant's brain suggest that even relative to its overall size it has a large hippocampus, the component in the mammalian brain linked to memory and an important part of its limbic system, which is involved in processing emotions.There has been (i) __________ elephant's fabled mental capacities until recently, when these behavioral observations have begun to be (ii) __________ by brain science. MRI scans of an elephant's brain suggest that even relative to its overall size it has a large hippocampus, the component in the mammalian brain linked to memory and an important part of its limbic system, which is involved in processing emotions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　only anecdotal evidence for; buttressed
observation
observation: standard unit headword
#216 TC TRIPLE
The general claim that artists are the preeminent specialists in (i) __________ is refuted by the obvious fact that the great mathematical and scientific discoveries of the past and present have been as much the result of (ii) __________ thinking as have great (iii) __________ discoveries.The general claim that artists are the preeminent specialists in (i) __________ is refuted by the obvious fact that the great mathematical and scientific discoveries of the past and present have been as much the result of (ii) __________ thinking as have great (iii) __________ discoveries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　creativity; original; artistic
original
original: standard unit headword
#217 TC SINGLE
Those who had once been his __________ within the bureaucracy, responsive to his every string-pulling maneuver, were now distressingly prone to independent judgment, and amicable enough but with an infuriating, vaguely superior manner.Those who had once been his __________ within the bureaucracy, responsive to his every string-pulling maneuver, were now distressingly prone to independent judgment, and amicable enough but with an infuriating, vaguely superior manner.
答案：D　marionettes
prone
prone: standard unit headword
#218 SE
The physics graveyard is strewn with the skeletons of failed theories, unexplained effects, and anomalous particles that briefly __________ the research spotlight, then rapidly fade from view.The physics graveyard is strewn with the skeletons of failed theories, unexplained effects, and anomalous particles that briefly __________ the research spotlight, then rapidly fade from view.
答案：D, F　capture; secure
effect
effect: standard unit headword
#219 TC DOUBLE
After peaking around 3.5 million years ago, the number of large mammalian carnivore species declined gradually over the next million and a half years or so, mostly because the rate at which new species (i) __________ slowed down while the extinction rate (ii) __________.After peaking around 3.5 million years ago, the number of large mammalian carnivore species declined gradually over the next million and a half years or so, mostly because the rate at which new species (i) __________ slowed down while the extinction rate (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　originated; remained largely unchanged
originate
originate: standard unit headword
#220 TC SINGLE
Melinda's political beliefs were a matter of __________: she held true only to those positions that served her own best interest.Melinda's political beliefs were a matter of __________: she held true only to those positions that served her own best interest.
答案：C　expediency
interest
interest: standard unit headword
#221 SE
Individuals interested in longevity have sought to fine-tune their bodies with all kinds of __________ diets: only raw foods, only plants, only the flesh, fruit, and nuts that prehistoric humans would have hunted and foraged.Individuals interested in longevity have sought to fine-tune their bodies with all kinds of __________ diets: only raw foods, only plants, only the flesh, fruit, and nuts that prehistoric humans would have hunted and foraged.
答案：D, F　proscriptive; exacting
interest
interest: standard unit headword
#222 TC TRIPLE
The recent finding is particularly (i) __________, given that climatologists' predictions about sea-level changes are based on the expectation that if the ice sheet lost mass at its edges, its overall mass would increase, since rising temperatures would lead to more snowfall over the continent's midsection. However, if overall mass is actually (ii) __________, it will mean that climatologists' predictions for the rise in the sea level for the coming century are (iii) __________.The recent finding is particularly (i) __________, given that climatologists' predictions about sea-level changes are based on the expectation that if the ice sheet lost mass at its edges, its overall mass would increase, since rising temperatures would lead to more snowfall over the continent's midsection. However, if overall mass is actually (ii) __________, it will mean that climatologists' predictions for the rise in the sea level for the coming century are (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　ominous; decreasing; seriously understated
understate
understate: standard unit headword

The new drug was useful, but unfortunately its effect was largely (i)__________ rather than (ii)__________.The new drug was useful, but unfortunately its effect was largely (i)__________ rather than (ii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　palliative; curative
effect
effect: standard unit headword
#224 TC SINGLE
The effects of sea-level rise that followed the last ice age were not __________ since they were dependent on local and regional tectonic and other geological effects.The effects of sea-level rise that followed the last ice age were not __________ since they were dependent on local and regional tectonic and other geological effects.
答案：D　homogeneous
effect
effect: standard unit headword
#225 SE
In politics, tactical calculations about which groups a candidate should appeal to are never pleasant, but they are not always __________, and sometimes they are necessary.In politics, tactical calculations about which groups a candidate should appeal to are never pleasant, but they are not always __________, and sometimes they are necessary.
答案：B, D　sleazy; sordid
tactical
tactical: standard unit headword
#226 TC DOUBLE
The committee called for restrictions not just on chemicals that are hazardous according to the standard definition (which equates hazard with toxicity) but on all chemicals that are persistent and that accumulate in the biosphere. Toxicity is an (i) __________ criterion, the committee argued, since evidence of toxicity (ii) __________ public health.The committee called for restrictions not just on chemicals that are hazardous according to the standard definition (which equates hazard with toxicity) but on all chemicals that are persistent and that accumulate in the biosphere. Toxicity is an (i) __________ criterion, the committee argued, since evidence of toxicity (ii) __________ public health.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　inadequate; can emerge too late to protect
emerge
emerge: standard unit headword
#227 TC TRIPLE
Biologists have little (i)__________ drawing the link between the success of humanity and human (ii)__________. Indeed, many biologists claim that this attribute, the ability to (iii)__________, or, to put it more sharply, to make individuals subordinate their self-interest to the needs of the group, lies at the root of human achievement.Biologists have little (i)__________ drawing the link between the success of humanity and human (ii)__________. Indeed, many biologists claim that this attribute, the ability to (iii)__________, or, to put it more sharply, to make individuals subordinate their self-interest to the needs of the group, lies at the root of human achievement.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　compunction about; sociability; cooperate

interest: standard unit headword
#228 TC SINGLE
For a small group of experts, the field is a lifelong obsession, but it is so __________ that it mystifies virtually everyone else.For a small group of experts, the field is a lifelong obsession, but it is so __________ that it mystifies virtually everyone else.
答案：A　arcane
virtually
virtually: standard unit headword
#229 SE
Smith's analysis of climate change is even more __________ than that supplied by most well-informed writers, and thus deserves serious consideration.Smith's analysis of climate change is even more __________ than that supplied by most well-informed writers, and thus deserves serious consideration.
答案：A, C　nuanced; complex
nuance
nuance: standard unit headword
#230 TC TRIPLE
When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted towards positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more often considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the established view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted towards positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more often considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the established view.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine
emerge
emerge: standard unit headword
#231 TC DOUBLE
To abolish the existence of nation-states is neither feasible nor desirable; but insofar as there are collective interests that transcend national boundaries, the (i) __________ of nation-states must be (ii) __________ to international institutions.To abolish the existence of nation-states is neither feasible nor desirable; but insofar as there are collective interests that transcend national boundaries, the (i) __________ of nation-states must be (ii) __________ to international institutions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　sovereignty; subordinated

interest: standard unit headword

Aside from nutrient availability, growth rate is the primary determinant of size in single-celled organisms: rapidly growing Escherichia coli cells are more than twice as large as their __________ counterparts.Aside from nutrient availability, growth rate is the primary determinant of size in single-celled organisms: rapidly growing Escherichia coli cells are more than twice as large as their __________ counterparts.
答案：C, E　more sluggish; slower

sluggish: standard unit headword

In the 1980s the press release announcing the discovery of a new fundamental particle turned a once __________ hypothesis of interest to no more than a few hundred physicists into an undisputed fact of global significance.In the 1980s the press release announcing the discovery of a new fundamental particle turned a once __________ hypothesis of interest to no more than a few hundred physicists into an undisputed fact of global significance.
答案：C　recondite

This judgment cohered with the high level of mathematical theory, which (ii)__________ mathematical computation together with empirical observations as (iii)__________ of science and denied any role to speculative hypotheses of a strongly theoretical nature.The historian of ancient science Otto Neugebauer concluded that Babylonian astronomical texts are (i)__________ because everything has been eliminated from the astronomy except observations and the mathematical consequences of an initial hypothesis about the fundamental character of the astronomical movements. This judgment cohered with the high level of mathematical theory, which (ii)__________ mathematical computation together with empirical observations as (iii)__________ of science and denied any role to speculative hypotheses of a strongly theoretical nature.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　scientific; admitted; necessary characteristics

Blank 2

答案：D, E　anticipate; prefigure

答案：E　superior to

There were few (ii)__________, however, so the form (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　a propitious; practical alternatives; persisted

The overall message of this book on neuroscience is (i) __________: the brain is quite (ii) __________, and therefore damaged brains can be healed, aging brains can be rejuvenated, and even ordinarily healthy brains can be made faster and better.The overall message of this book on neuroscience is (i) __________: the brain is quite (ii) __________, and therefore damaged brains can be healed, aging brains can be rejuvenated, and even ordinarily healthy brains can be made faster and better.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　upbeat; malleable

#240 TC SINGLE
Apocalyptic ideology can be __________ in its effects, since those who predict the end of the world have often been great innovators: the fear of catastrophe, despite its perceived inevitability, frequently acts as a spur to construction and renewal.Apocalyptic ideology can be __________ in its effects, since those who predict the end of the world have often been great innovators: the fear of catastrophe, despite its perceived inevitability, frequently acts as a spur to construction and renewal.
答案：C　paradoxical

答案：C, E　obviate; displace

Blank 2

答案：E　inertia

Jawaharlal Nehru, by contrast, has been subjected to (ii) __________ that border on caricature, portrayed as (iii) __________ a one-dimensional view of science and a vacuous universalism.In recent years in India, the works of such figures as Mohandas Gandhi, Vallabhbhai Patel, Subhas Chandra Bose, and Rabindranath Tagore have all benefited from (i) __________ interpretations of each man's life and thought. 答案：B, D　nuance; subtlety

#250 TC TRIPLE
Viewing people as "social atoms" that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another--in an effort to avoid collisions--and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.Viewing people as "social atoms" that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another--in an effort to avoid collisions--and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　lawlike regulations; chaotic; self-perpetuating

Blank 2

答案：D, F　soft; malleable

答案：C　paradigmatic

However, other scholars emphasize the (i)__________ of this view, empirically demonstrating that while economic development does in fact contribute to the well-being of the population of developing countries, the magnitude of development's positive effects on well-being has (ii)__________. In other words, these scholars suggest that (iii)__________ economic development and human well-being is taking place in developing countries.Firebaugh and Beck contend that economic development improves the overall well-being of people within developing countries. However, other scholars emphasize the (i)__________ of this view, empirically demonstrating that while economic development does in fact contribute to the well-being of the population of developing countries, the magnitude of development's positive effects on well-being has (ii)__________. In other words, these scholars suggest that (iii)__________ economic development and human well-being is taking place in developing countries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　limitation; decreased over time; a decoupling of

Even though the idea that medical research should be rigorously objective is (i) __________ one in the medical community, the editors of medical journals often display a disquieting (ii) __________ when it comes to articles submitted by researchers who accept money from the makers of the products they evaluate in their research; editors rarely ask whether that research is truly disinterested.Even though the idea that medical research should be rigorously objective is (i) __________ one in the medical community, the editors of medical journals often display a disquieting (ii) __________ when it comes to articles submitted by researchers who accept money from the makers of the products they evaluate in their research; editors rarely ask whether that research is truly disinterested.

Blank 2
答案：B, E　an uncontroversial; credulity

Blank 1
Blank 2

#259 TC TRIPLE
For a time in the early Middle Ages, Latin culture came close to (i)__________: the witness to that is the (ii)__________ of manuscript copies of texts datable to the period. The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)__________, a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to use forever.For a time in the early Middle Ages, Latin culture came close to (i)__________: the witness to that is the (ii)__________ of manuscript copies of texts datable to the period. The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)__________, a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to use forever.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　vanishing; paucity; virtually came to a standstill

答案：A, B　minimal; trifling

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　oversimplified; virtually unknown

#263 TC TRIPLE
Evidence has been accumulating since the 1930s that reducing an animal's energy intake below its energy expenditure extends the life span and delays the (i)__________ of age-related diseases in rats, dogs, fish, and monkeys. Such results have inspired thousands of people to (ii)__________ in the hope of living longer, healthier lives. They have also led to a search for drugs that (iii)__________ the effects of calorie restriction without the pain of actually going on a diet.Evidence has been accumulating since the 1930s that reducing an animal's energy intake below its energy expenditure extends the life span and delays the (i)__________ of age-related diseases in rats, dogs, fish, and monkeys. Such results have inspired thousands of people to (ii)__________ in the hope of living longer, healthier lives. They have also led to a search for drugs that (iii)__________ the effects of calorie restriction without the pain of actually going on a diet.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　onset; put up with constant hunger; mimic
effect
effect: standard unit headword

答案：A　impediment to
