precedent: standard unit headword
#265 SE
It’s a sign of John Dramani Mahama’s maturity as a writer that he is willing to consider his country’s future so __________: his memoir is appealingly honest, given to clear-eyed assessments rather than exaggerated accounts of achievements.It’s a sign of John Dramani Mahama’s maturity as a writer that he is willing to consider his country’s future so __________: his memoir is appealingly honest, given to clear-eyed assessments rather than exaggerated accounts of achievements.
答案：B, E　dispassionately; analytically
memoir
memoir: standard unit headword
#266 TC DOUBLE
In the mid-1990s, researchers were aware that individuals and subpopulations vary in their susceptibilities to environmental pollutants. However, environmental health scientists, hoping to identify general trends, tended to view these variations as (i) __________ and used methods, in both human and animal studies, to (ii) __________.In the mid-1990s, researchers were aware that individuals and subpopulations vary in their susceptibilities to environmental pollutants. However, environmental health scientists, hoping to identify general trends, tended to view these variations as (i) __________ and used methods, in both human and animal studies, to (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　distractions; minimize their effect
effect
effect: standard unit headword
#267 TC TRIPLE
So (i)__________ is the reputation of the country's police for corruption and other forms of (ii)__________ that it has become a kind of tradition that every newly appointed police chief pledges to (iii)__________ the force.So (i)__________ is the reputation of the country's police for corruption and other forms of (ii)__________ that it has become a kind of tradition that every newly appointed police chief pledges to (iii)__________ the force.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　paralyzing; criminality; reform
corruption
corruption: standard unit headword
#268 TC SINGLE
Unlike some mammals—cows and sheep, for instance—that are notably __________, lions have a wide range of facial expressions.Unlike some mammals—cows and sheep, for instance—that are notably __________, lions have a wide range of facial expressions.
答案：B　impassive
sluggish
sluggish: standard unit headword
#269 SE
Isabel DiVanna suggests that the ability of scholars to impose on the poem the lessons they wish it to convey depends on its __________ nature.Isabel DiVanna suggests that the ability of scholars to impose on the poem the lessons they wish it to convey depends on its __________ nature.
答案：B, D　pliant; malleable
malleable
malleable: standard unit headword
#270 TC TRIPLE
(i)__________ have often shrilled that Australia's Great Barrier Reef is dying, a result of agricultural runoff from the (ii)__________ Queensland coast. In truth, the preservation of the reef (iii)__________, a combination of active government intervention and the beneficial effects of responsible tourism.(i)__________ have often shrilled that Australia's Great Barrier Reef is dying, a result of agricultural runoff from the (ii)__________ Queensland coast. In truth, the preservation of the reef (iii)__________, a combination of active government intervention and the beneficial effects of responsible tourism.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　Scaremongers; over-farmed; is something of a success story
effect
effect: standard unit headword
#271 TC DOUBLE
In a sharp blow to his reputation as (i) __________ leader, the evidence that the mayor has recently been involved in malfeasance seems to be (ii) __________.In a sharp blow to his reputation as (i) __________ leader, the evidence that the mayor has recently been involved in malfeasance seems to be (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　an exemplary; sound
malfeasance
malfeasance: standard unit headword
#272 SE
Looking back on a project that they had approached with both great __________ and considerable aspirations, they were amused to recognize that neither the fears nor the hopes had been at all realistic.Looking back on a project that they had approached with both great __________ and considerable aspirations, they were amused to recognize that neither the fears nor the hopes had been at all realistic.
答案：D, E　trepidation; misgivings
misgiving
misgiving: standard unit headword
#273 TC SINGLE
Many creative photographers were delighted to find in instant photography a mode that encouraged them to stop viewing photography as __________ and start viewing it as something they could handle with spontaneity, even derision.Many creative photographers were delighted to find in instant photography a mode that encouraged them to stop viewing photography as __________ and start viewing it as something they could handle with spontaneity, even derision.
答案：A　sacrosanct
malleable
malleable: standard unit headword
#274 TC TRIPLE
Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also a misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons is that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)__________, communal devil feeding is (ii)__________ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)__________ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also a misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons is that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)__________, communal devil feeding is (ii)__________ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)__________ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　free-for-all; structured; ritualized
purposeful
purposeful: standard unit headword
#275 TC DOUBLE
Catherine the Great comes across in her memoirs as (i) __________ ruler with a razor-sharp intellect, letting nothing stand in the way of her ambitions. In short, the impression the memoirs give is entirely in accord with her reputation for being (ii) __________.Catherine the Great comes across in her memoirs as (i) __________ ruler with a razor-sharp intellect, letting nothing stand in the way of her ambitions. In short, the impression the memoirs give is entirely in accord with her reputation for being (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　an uncompromising; formidable
memoir
memoir: standard unit headword
#276 SE
Modern historians' silence on British views of French empire-building is perhaps explained, at least in part, by the __________ of Victorian-era scholarship on pre-1870 French colonial enterprise.Modern historians' silence on British views of French empire-building is perhaps explained, at least in part, by the __________ of Victorian-era scholarship on pre-1870 French colonial enterprise.
答案：A, F　paucity; scarcity
paucity
paucity: standard unit headword

The province has long claimed to be just another developing region, even as its economy __________ those of other regions and threatens to eclipse the rest of the country combined.The province has long claimed to be just another developing region, even as its economy __________ those of other regions and threatens to eclipse the rest of the country combined.
答案：D　outstrips
engage
engage: standard unit headword
#278 TC TRIPLE
Women in the mining towns of the American West were strictly stereotyped into neat categories of public and private, good and bad, but the 100 intrepid female prospectors in Zanjani's book managed to (i)__________ those categories. In addition to providing documentation that demolishes the all-male version of prospecting, Zanjani uses the examples of her female loners to (ii)__________ some of the (iii)__________ generalizations about Euro-American women as uniformly nurturant and sociable pioneers.Women in the mining towns of the American West were strictly stereotyped into neat categories of public and private, good and bad, but the 100 intrepid female prospectors in Zanjani's book managed to (i)__________ those categories. In addition to providing documentation that demolishes the all-male version of prospecting, Zanjani uses the examples of her female loners to (ii)__________ some of the (iii)__________ generalizations about Euro-American women as uniformly nurturant and sociable pioneers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　confound; puncture; facile
sociable
sociable: standard unit headword
#279 TC DOUBLE
The Newseum is a museum created by, for, and about journalists and newshounds. But for all its celebration of the news industry, its creators have taken care to avoid (i) __________. Along with the many testimonials to journalistic courage and a memorial to journalists who lost their lives on the job, the museum presents noteworthy examples of journalistic (ii) __________.The Newseum is a museum created by, for, and about journalists and newshounds. But for all its celebration of the news industry, its creators have taken care to avoid (i) __________. Along with the many testimonials to journalistic courage and a memorial to journalists who lost their lives on the job, the museum presents noteworthy examples of journalistic (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　puffery; malfeasance
malfeasance
malfeasance: standard unit headword
#280 TC SINGLE
In his youth the naturalist and artist James Audubon was given to __________ glamorous tales about himself: he falsely claimed to have studied under a renowned French painter and hinted that he was the heir apparent to the French throne.In his youth the naturalist and artist James Audubon was given to __________ glamorous tales about himself: he falsely claimed to have studied under a renowned French painter and hinted that he was the heir apparent to the French throne.
答案：E　concocting
understate
understate: standard unit headword
#281 SE
In its few decades of existence, the field of technology assessment has undergone large changes: its original high ambitions to predict consequences of technology have been __________ if not discarded.In its few decades of existence, the field of technology assessment has undergone large changes: its original high ambitions to predict consequences of technology have been __________ if not discarded.
答案：E, F　tempered; modulated
original
original: standard unit headword
#282 TC DOUBLE
Britain’s Queen Victoria, however (i)__________ she had been at the beginning of her reign, was politically much more (ii)__________ by the end of her time on the throne, as she resigned herself to the emergence of an increasing powerful electorate that prevented her from stubbornly insisting on getting her own way in matters of state.Britain’s Queen Victoria, however (i)__________ she had been at the beginning of her reign, was politically much more (ii)__________ by the end of her time on the throne, as she resigned herself to the emergence of an increasing powerful electorate that prevented her from stubbornly insisting on getting her own way in matters of state.

Blank 2
答案：C, F　obdurate; malleable

Blank 3

答案：A　significance

It remains a mystery how Theobroma cacao, which scholars believe originated in lowland Amazonia, was introduced to tropical Mesoarnerica. It must have been a (i)__________ process: cacao trees do not easily sprout from seed, need years to mature, and grow only in humid lowland forest. Moreover, in a tropical climate, cacao pods spoil quickly, rendering their transport from lowland Amazonia to tropical Mesoamerica in a single journey by foot or canoe (ii)__________.It remains a mystery how Theobroma cacao, which scholars believe originated in lowland Amazonia, was introduced to tropical Mesoarnerica. It must have been a (i)__________ process: cacao trees do not easily sprout from seed, need years to mature, and grow only in humid lowland forest. Moreover, in a tropical climate, cacao pods spoil quickly, rendering their transport from lowland Amazonia to tropical Mesoamerica in a single journey by foot or canoe (ii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　protracted; unlikely
originateprotracted

Despite the (i)__________ in negotiations apparently signaled by the recent agreement between the two neighboring countries, the countries remain (ii)__________ even about the import of that agreement. One wants to prolong agreed-to resumption of limited cross-border traffic, believing it can become entrenched as a new status quo. The other, by contrast, insists that the resumption is (iii)__________ and has been undertaken voluntarily and provisionally in order to show goodwill.Despite the (i)__________ in negotiations apparently signaled by the recent agreement between the two neighboring countries, the countries remain (ii)__________ even about the import of that agreement. One wants to prolong agreed-to resumption of limited cross-border traffic, believing it can become entrenched as a new status quo. The other, by contrast, insists that the resumption is (iii)__________ and has been undertaken voluntarily and provisionally in order to show goodwill.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, H　progress; unreconciled; temporary
prolong
prolong: standard unit headword

This conclusion would be (ii)__________, however, insofar as not all nostalgia necessarily results in a (iii)__________ of critical thought.Logically, one might assume that the nostalgia in which Guzman engages at times in his documentary about Chilean history would be (i)__________ critical reflection and thus leave the film intellectually flaccid. This conclusion would be (ii)__________, however, insofar as not all nostalgia necessarily results in a (iii)__________ of critical thought.

Blank 2

Their forms thus necessarily become (i) __________ over time and their original features are (ii) __________.Ancient cart ruts found on Malta were created in soft limestone that begins to dissolve when exposed to rainfall. Their forms thus necessarily become (i) __________ over time and their original features are (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　degraded; obscured

答案：D, F　coercive; imperious

Some fish farmers use upwards of five kilograms of wild fish as feed to grow one kilogram of carnivorous species like sea bass. Such (ii)__________ feed plus other environmental side effects undermine the expected (iii)__________ fish farming.Overall, fish farming may provide a (i)__________ wild fish stocks, but for certain species, the farmers cause a net loss of wild fish. Some fish farmers use upwards of five kilograms of wild fish as feed to grow one kilogram of carnivorous species like sea bass. Such (ii)__________ feed plus other environmental side effects undermine the expected (iii)__________ fish farming.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　supplement to; appetite for; benefits of

It was not until 1995 that a planet beyond our solar system was first sighted, a discovery that greatly excited astronomers. Now, observations had (ii) __________.It was not until 1995 that a planet beyond our solar system was first sighted, a discovery that greatly excited astronomers. Many had supposed that the processes that gave rise to our solar system were not (i) __________, and that there were other planets in the universe. Now, observations had (ii) __________.

Blank 2
答案：C, D　unique; caught up with belief

答案：B, D　fastidious; finicky

Gravitational waves - ripples in the geometry of space-time - are analogous to electromagnetic waves. The challenge in trying to observe these waves directly is that they are extremely weak. To make waves large enough to be (i)__________, the most (ii)__________ events in the universe are required: supernova explosions, the formation of black holes, or the collision of stars. Even so, the effects are (iii)__________. The geometry changes so little that a distance of several kilometers changes by less than the diameter of a proton.Gravitational waves - ripples in the geometry of space-time - are analogous to electromagnetic waves. The challenge in trying to observe these waves directly is that they are extremely weak. To make waves large enough to be (i)__________, the most (ii)__________ events in the universe are required: supernova explosions, the formation of black holes, or the collision of stars. Even so, the effects are (iii)__________. The geometry changes so little that a distance of several kilometers changes by less than the diameter of a proton.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　detectable; violent; minuscule

Blank 2
Blank 3

renaissance: standard unit headword | proliferation: standard unit headword
#317 TC SINGLE
Although tyrannosaurine dinosaurs have in the past been characterized as __________ feeders-using jaw power to bust into or through bones and then swallowing whatever piece came off-the truth is that they were capable of a more measured approach.Although tyrannosaurine dinosaurs have in the past been characterized as __________ feeders-using jaw power to bust into or through bones and then swallowing whatever piece came off-the truth is that they were capable of a more measured approach.
答案：A　crude
cunning
cunning: standard unit headword
#318 TC TRIPLE
Media stories about climate regularly use spokespeople from interest groups as sources, but what those individuals say is often (i)__________, citing results from scientific research in a highly (ii)__________ manner and (iii)__________ the caveats that are part of a full scientific assessment.Media stories about climate regularly use spokespeople from interest groups as sources, but what those individuals say is often (i)__________, citing results from scientific research in a highly (ii)__________ manner and (iii)__________ the caveats that are part of a full scientific assessment.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　tendentious; selective; omitting
interest
interest: standard unit headword
#319 TC DOUBLE
It remains a mystery how Theobroma cacao, which scholars believe originated in lowland Amazonia, was introduced to tropical Mesoamerica. It must have been a (i) __________ process: cacao trees do not easily sprout from seed, need years to mature, and grow only in humid lowland forest. Moreover, in a tropical climate, cacao pods spoil quickly, rendering their transport from lowland Amazonia to tropical Mesoamerica in a single journey by foot or canoe (ii) __________.It remains a mystery how Theobroma cacao, which scholars believe originated in lowland Amazonia, was introduced to tropical Mesoamerica. It must have been a (i) __________ process: cacao trees do not easily sprout from seed, need years to mature, and grow only in humid lowland forest. Moreover, in a tropical climate, cacao pods spoil quickly, rendering their transport from lowland Amazonia to tropical Mesoamerica in a single journey by foot or canoe (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　protracted; unlikely
originateprotracted
originate: standard unit headword | protracted: standard unit headword
#320 TC SINGLE
The Presley song was a groundbreaking rock and roll single, but to describe it as "a seminal text in American culture," as one author does, is surely __________.The Presley song was a groundbreaking rock and roll single, but to describe it as "a seminal text in American culture," as one author does, is surely __________.
答案：D　hyperbolic
astute
astute: standard unit headword
#321 SE
In order to cultivate new repertoire, the music industry is providing a hearing for previously __________ female composers.In order to cultivate new repertoire, the music industry is providing a hearing for previously __________ female composers.
答案：E, F　anonymous; obscure
cultivate
cultivate: standard unit headword
#322 TC DOUBLE
There is a somewhat (i) __________ trend emerging in property law today: though property law historically has been used to legitimize the conquest of indigenous lands, indigenous groups worldwide are now (ii) __________ this same body of law to lay claim to their own cultural resources.There is a somewhat (i) __________ trend emerging in property law today: though property law historically has been used to legitimize the conquest of indigenous lands, indigenous groups worldwide are now (ii) __________ this same body of law to lay claim to their own cultural resources.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　ironic; employing
emergeironic
emerge: standard unit headword | ironic: standard unit headword
#323 TC TRIPLE
Climate change is posited to be a frequent factor in mass extinctions by some scientists who speculate that minor climate shifts can trigger a catastrophic cascade of effects. Although this pattern is (i)__________ in that it confirms warnings about our own possibly dire future, it is also somehow (ii)__________ as it sets forth a context for today's turbulent weather as (iii)__________ in a natural, cyclical process.Climate change is posited to be a frequent factor in mass extinctions by some scientists who speculate that minor climate shifts can trigger a catastrophic cascade of effects. Although this pattern is (i)__________ in that it confirms warnings about our own possibly dire future, it is also somehow (ii)__________ as it sets forth a context for today's turbulent weather as (iii)__________ in a natural, cyclical process.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　alarming; reassuring; unexceptional
effect
effect: standard unit headword
#324 TC SINGLE
Whereas water on Mars could plausibly have been delivered by repeated comet impacts in the geologically recent past, the ice on smaller bodies is more difficult to attribute to __________ source.Whereas water on Mars could plausibly have been delivered by repeated comet impacts in the geologically recent past, the ice on smaller bodies is more difficult to attribute to __________ source.
答案：A　an exogenous
terrestrial
terrestrial: standard unit headword
#325 SE
While normal floods resulting from usual monsoon rainfall are __________ the grows of crops, recently there has been an increase in the frequency high intensity floods that do not have such welcome effects.While normal floods resulting from usual monsoon rainfall are __________ the grows of crops, recently there has been an increase in the frequency high intensity floods that do not have such welcome effects.
答案：E, F　essential for; indispensable to

effect: standard unit headword
#326 TC TRIPLE
For whom is Shipton's A New History of Jazz intended? It is written in (i)__________ language and contains no extended musical analysis, suggesting that it is meant for amateurs. But as it is twice as long as the next-longest history of the subject, my guess is that general readers will be put off by its (ii)__________. On the other hand, its great length is precisely what enables Shipton to avoid (iii)__________ the story of jazz's development.For whom is Shipton's A New History of Jazz intended? It is written in (i)__________ language and contains no extended musical analysis, suggesting that it is meant for amateurs. But as it is twice as long as the next-longest history of the subject, my guess is that general readers will be put off by its (ii)__________. On the other hand, its great length is precisely what enables Shipton to avoid (iii)__________ the story of jazz's development.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　accessible; proliferation of detail; oversimplifying
paucityproliferation
paucity: standard unit headword | proliferation: standard unit headword

In general memoirs focus sharply on particular, and particularly memorable, events rather than on the entire life. This (i) __________ structure inevitably emphasizes drama at the expense of (ii) __________.In general memoirs focus sharply on particular, and particularly memorable, events rather than on the entire life. This (i) __________ structure inevitably emphasizes drama at the expense of (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　episodic; completeness
memoir
memoir: standard unit headword
#328 TC SINGLE
Nociception is the process of detection and processing of a noxious stimulus by the nervous system; however, nociception is not __________ pain, as the former is not necessarily associated with a negative emotional response or feeling.Nociception is the process of detection and processing of a noxious stimulus by the nervous system; however, nociception is not __________ pain, as the former is not necessarily associated with a negative emotional response or feeling.
答案：B　identical to
precede
precede: standard unit headword

Others have replied to this with variation on the theme of how we still hold footraces despite cars going much faster, but this analogy is (ii) __________ since cars do not help humans run faster while chess computers undoubtedly have an effect on the (iii) __________ of human chess.Among chess aficionados, there have been many doomsday scenarios about people (i) __________ the game as a result of the superiority of computer chess programs over human players. Others have replied to this with variation on the theme of how we still hold footraces despite cars going much faster, but this analogy is (ii) __________ since cars do not help humans run faster while chess computers undoubtedly have an effect on the (iii) __________ of human chess.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　losing interest in; spurious; quality

答案：D, F　realism; verisimilitude

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　amusing; compensate for

答案：B, F　placatory; conciliatory

答案：B　cacophonous

Yet the term "error" could be (i)__________ in that it suggests that there might be (ii)__________ way in which cognition should work. Such a binary opposition is (iii)__________.Human brains are amazing organs but they are not perfect. Cognitive biases, as we know, sometimes produce errors. Yet the term "error" could be (i)__________ in that it suggests that there might be (ii)__________ way in which cognition should work. Such a binary opposition is (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　problematic; a uniquely correct; reductive

The specimen is a fish with features that (ii) __________ the emergence of land animals and is thus a (iii) __________ of amphibians, reptiles, dinosaurs, and mammals.The newly discovered fossil skeletons have the fins, scales, and other attributes of a giant fish, but also the telling anatomical traits of (i) __________ creature, such as the beginnings of digits, protowrists, and joints resembling elbows toward the forward fins. The specimen is a fish with features that (ii) __________ the emergence of land animals and is thus a (iii) __________ of amphibians, reptiles, dinosaurs, and mammals.

#345 TC TRIPLE
It appears that the terrestrial biosphere has been absorbing a significant fraction of anthropogenic CO2, thereby slowing down the rate of global climate change. However, the (i) __________ of this (ii) __________ is not (iii) __________: if pushed too far, the biosphere might become a net source of CO2, thereby accelerating climate change and causing a positive feedback loop.It appears that the terrestrial biosphere has been absorbing a significant fraction of anthropogenic CO2, thereby slowing down the rate of global climate change. However, the (i) __________ of this (ii) __________ is not (iii) __________: if pushed too far, the biosphere might become a net source of CO2, thereby accelerating climate change and causing a positive feedback loop.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　continuation; compensatory mechanism; guaranteed
terrestrial
terrestrial: standard unit headword
#346 TC DOUBLE
An architect of Koolhaas' (i) __________ might plausibly prefer designing on paper to building in concrete. Remaining within the realm of his own imagination, he need not worry about pesky clients who can dilute a project into mediocrity. But in fact what Koolhaas enjoys most is the opportunity to engage with a client. When asked if he would consider designing a house for himself, he replied that the idea bored him, that it would feel too (ii) __________. For him, the whole point of architecture is the encounter with the other.An architect of Koolhaas' (i) __________ might plausibly prefer designing on paper to building in concrete. Remaining within the realm of his own imagination, he need not worry about pesky clients who can dilute a project into mediocrity. But in fact what Koolhaas enjoys most is the opportunity to engage with a client. When asked if he would consider designing a house for himself, he replied that the idea bored him, that it would feel too (ii) __________. For him, the whole point of architecture is the encounter with the other.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　unfettered originality; solipsistic
engage
engage: standard unit headword

答案：B, F　shortcoming; fault
interest
interest: standard unit headword

When learners are engaged in mathematical problem-solving, their activity does not have from the outset the structure of what will be considered a mathematical proof. Instead, a tangle of intuitions, know-how, factual knowledge, and a variety of other mental constructs allows learners to entertain choices and make decisions. Nonetheless, (i) __________ such mental activity (ii) __________ is (iii) __________: in many situations this process works, although it can prove to be wrong as a result of overgeneralizations.When learners are engaged in mathematical problem-solving, their activity does not have from the outset the structure of what will be considered a mathematical proof. Instead, a tangle of intuitions, know-how, factual knowledge, and a variety of other mental constructs allows learners to entertain choices and make decisions. Nonetheless, (i) __________ such mental activity (ii) __________ is (iii) __________: in many situations this process works, although it can prove to be wrong as a result of overgeneralizations.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　describing; as confused; uncharitable

engage: standard unit headword

Blank 2

Rather, before the advent of modern public health systems, (iii)__________ the unpleasantness of the disease and its treatment was seen as a way to ensure that the public remained vigilant against possible sources of contagion.The (i)__________ in local coverage of a rabies outbreak in colonial Hanoi may be startling today, but it cannot be attributed solely to the (ii)__________ of the era's journalists. Any of those behaviors might also help explain why these people have lower disease rates.In general, observational studies tend to (i) __________ the effect of diet on overall health because a healthy diet is often (ii) __________ a healthy lifestyle. People who watch what they eat probably exercise more, avoid smoking, and follow other behaviors (iii) __________ good health. Any of those behaviors might also help explain why these people have lower disease rates.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　overstate; a marker for; linked to

Blank 2

The scientific study of laughter faces a frustrating paradox: ideal experiments take place in rigidly controlled environments with all but the most salient variables eliminated, but such sterile settings are often __________ laughter.The scientific study of laughter faces a frustrating paradox: ideal experiments take place in rigidly controlled environments with all but the most salient variables eliminated, but such sterile settings are often __________ laughter.
答案：A, D　inimical to; adverse to
all but

#362 TC TRIPLE
Some scientists speculate that changes in atmospheric methane were responsible for some abrupt global warming events in the past such as the one at the end of the geologic period known as the Younger Dryas. There is good evidence that large quantities of methane have been released quickly in the past through natural processes; however, methane is a relatively (i)__________ molecule in the atmosphere and thus (ii)__________ through chemical reactions with other compounds. The effect of even a large increase would therefore be (iii)__________.Some scientists speculate that changes in atmospheric methane were responsible for some abrupt global warming events in the past such as the one at the end of the geologic period known as the Younger Dryas. There is good evidence that large quantities of methane have been released quickly in the past through natural processes; however, methane is a relatively (i)__________ molecule in the atmosphere and thus (ii)__________ through chemical reactions with other compounds. The effect of even a large increase would therefore be (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　unstable; fairly quickly destroyed; ephemeral

effect: standard unit headword

In a newly formed group of people, the hierarchy that emerges may be entirely __________, but group members will nevertheless tend to construct after-the-fact rationalizations for why those in charge should be in charge.In a newly formed group of people, the hierarchy that emerges may be entirely __________, but group members will nevertheless tend to construct after-the-fact rationalizations for why those in charge should be in charge.
答案：B, D　haphazard; arbitrary

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　duplicative; muddies

答案：B, F, G　documents; imagination; fanciful

memoir: standard unit headword
#366 SE
It is an ironic reversal that just those politicians who most vociferously lambasted the distorting complexities of the country's tax system are now the ones __________ an agreement that worsens the mess.It is an ironic reversal that just those politicians who most vociferously lambasted the distorting complexities of the country's tax system are now the ones __________ an agreement that worsens the mess.
答案：A, D　espousing; championing
ironic
ironic: standard unit headword
#367 TC DOUBLE
In writing about environmental issues, the author takes on some special artistic challenges. It is hard to write a good novel that takes a strong stand on a social issue, although there are a few novelists, Zola and Norris for example, who do manage to (i) __________ the often circumscribing effects of (ii) __________.In writing about environmental issues, the author takes on some special artistic challenges. It is hard to write a good novel that takes a strong stand on a social issue, although there are a few novelists, Zola and Norris for example, who do manage to (i) __________ the often circumscribing effects of (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　transcend; polemic
effect
effect: standard unit headword
#368 TC TRIPLE
In some countries, filibustering and stall tactics have held improvements to air and water standards at bay, leaving environmental activists (i) __________ . Moreover, Earth Day--a global holiday meant to celebrate the wonder of life on the planet--has (ii) __________ the frivolous "picnic holidays," its significance (iii) __________ the smoke of barbecue pits and backyard grills.In some countries, filibustering and stall tactics have held improvements to air and water standards at bay, leaving environmental activists (i) __________ . Moreover, Earth Day--a global holiday meant to celebrate the wonder of life on the planet--has (ii) __________ the frivolous "picnic holidays," its significance (iii) __________ the smoke of barbecue pits and backyard grills.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　dispirited; joined the ranks of; obscured by
activist
activist: standard unit headword
#369 SE
Although anecdotes about her abound, it is difficult to verify facts about al-Khansa'--like many early poetic figures, she may well be a composite figure, her corpus __________ the works of many women poets.Although anecdotes about her abound, it is difficult to verify facts about al-Khansa'--like many early poetic figures, she may well be a composite figure, her corpus __________ the works of many women poets.
答案：B, F　an amalgamation of; a fusion of

anecdote: standard unit headword
#370 TC DOUBLE
Observations from the Chandra X-ray Observatory give astronomers even more evidence that galaxies (i) __________, and that even their central supermassive black holes eventually (ii) __________.Observations from the Chandra X-ray Observatory give astronomers even more evidence that galaxies (i) __________, and that even their central supermassive black holes eventually (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　coalesce; merge
observation
observation: standard unit headword
#371 SE
Interest in the recovery of lost works by Brazilian women writers is growing as scholars increasingly recognize writing by Brazilian women as inherently worth study, not as __________ writing by Brazilian men.Interest in the recovery of lost works by Brazilian women writers is growing as scholars increasingly recognize writing by Brazilian women as inherently worth study, not as __________ writing by Brazilian men.
答案：B, E　ancillary to; subsidiary to
interest
interest: standard unit headword
#372 TC TRIPLE
The authors considered how it can be that the fashion industry continues to (i) __________ when there are no laws that prevent one designer or company from copying another designer's original idea. They came to the conclusion that the fashion industry (ii) __________ the piracy of new designs: design copying contributes to a process of induced obsolescence by diffusing new designs into the mainstream, causing them to lose their insider appeal. A market for new designs is thus (iii) __________.The authors considered how it can be that the fashion industry continues to (i) __________ when there are no laws that prevent one designer or company from copying another designer's original idea. They came to the conclusion that the fashion industry (ii) __________ the piracy of new designs: design copying contributes to a process of induced obsolescence by diffusing new designs into the mainstream, causing them to lose their insider appeal. A market for new designs is thus (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　prosper; benefits from; perpetuated
original
original: standard unit headword
#373 TC DOUBLE
It is hard to know exactly what is causing a worldwide proliferation of jellyfish, but we can speculate. The fishing industry has (i) __________ populations of jellyfish predators, such as red tuna, swordfish, and sea turtles. And when small, plankton-eating fish, such as anchovies, are (ii) __________, jellyfish flourish, gorging on plankton and reproducing prolifically.It is hard to know exactly what is causing a worldwide proliferation of jellyfish, but we can speculate. The fishing industry has (i) __________ populations of jellyfish predators, such as red tuna, swordfish, and sea turtles. And when small, plankton-eating fish, such as anchovies, are (ii) __________, jellyfish flourish, gorging on plankton and reproducing prolifically.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　depleted; overharvested
proliferation
proliferation: standard unit headword
#374 TC TRIPLE
Although the impression that quantum mechanics is limited to the microworld (i) __________ the public understanding of science, it is (ii) __________. Quantum effects may be harder to see in the macroworld, but experiments have now confirmed that these effects are (iii) __________ than anyone ever suspected.Although the impression that quantum mechanics is limited to the microworld (i) __________ the public understanding of science, it is (ii) __________. Quantum effects may be harder to see in the macroworld, but experiments have now confirmed that these effects are (iii) __________ than anyone ever suspected.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　permeates; erroneous; more pervasive
effect
effect: standard unit headword
#375 SE
Researchers who study pollinator behavior are increasingly interested in multicomponent floral signals, but they often focus on how individual components are learned by pollinators rather than on the benefit of signal __________ per se.Researchers who study pollinator behavior are increasingly interested in multicomponent floral signals, but they often focus on how individual components are learned by pollinators rather than on the benefit of signal __________ per se.
答案：A, F　complexity; intricacy
interest
interest: standard unit headword
#376 TC DOUBLE
That there were a dozen complex debates on the lawyer's mind at any given moment did not (i) __________ his ability to perform physical work; on the contrary, he labored more vigorously when simultaneously engaged in such (ii) __________ forensic exercises.That there were a dozen complex debates on the lawyer's mind at any given moment did not (i) __________ his ability to perform physical work; on the contrary, he labored more vigorously when simultaneously engaged in such (ii) __________ forensic exercises.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　impede; cerebral

engage: standard unit headword
#377 TC TRIPLE
That the ancient Greeks did not understand tides is (i) __________: for the Greeks, and the Romans after them, the tidal oscillations of the Mediterranean were so (ii) __________ that they were frequently (iii) __________ the effects of local winds and weather.That the ancient Greeks did not understand tides is (i) __________: for the Greeks, and the Romans after them, the tidal oscillations of the Mediterranean were so (ii) __________ that they were frequently (iii) __________ the effects of local winds and weather.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　unsurprising; minimal; obscured by

effect: standard unit headword
#378 SE
While lunar geology may appear to be a relatively __________ area of planetary science, it is important to realize that the Moon's surface and interior retain records that shed light on the histories of all terrestrial planets.While lunar geology may appear to be a relatively __________ area of planetary science, it is important to realize that the Moon's surface and interior retain records that shed light on the histories of all terrestrial planets.
答案：A, C　parochial; narrow

#379 TC DOUBLE

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　singularity; exaggerated
precede

The artists sought to (i) __________ precisely the impulses that traditional or generally accepted thinking had (ii) __________. By cultivating instinctive, irrational awarenesses--which might come from dreams or from accepting results derived by chance or from a receptivity to taboo--they hoped to (iii) __________ their audience's sense of everyday reality.The artists sought to (i) __________ precisely the impulses that traditional or generally accepted thinking had (ii) __________. By cultivating instinctive, irrational awarenesses--which might come from dreams or from accepting results derived by chance or from a receptivity to taboo--they hoped to (iii) __________ their audience's sense of everyday reality.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　affirm; marginalized; unsettle

#381 SE
How much corruption exists in internationally funded development projects is a matter of __________, though one well-supported estimate is that it might affect 40 percent or more of such projects.How much corruption exists in internationally funded development projects is a matter of __________, though one well-supported estimate is that it might affect 40 percent or more of such projects.
答案：C, E　speculation; conjecture
corruption
corruption: standard unit headword
#382 TC DOUBLE
The nineteenth-century periodical The Farmer's Wife positioned the farm woman as a consumer, as evidenced by its echoing the (i) __________ sensibilities of consumer-oriented magazines such as Ladies' Home Journal, but in reality the farm woman was often engaged in (ii) __________ as much as in consumption, planting and harvesting alongside other workers in the field.The nineteenth-century periodical The Farmer's Wife positioned the farm woman as a consumer, as evidenced by its echoing the (i) __________ sensibilities of consumer-oriented magazines such as Ladies' Home Journal, but in reality the farm woman was often engaged in (ii) __________ as much as in consumption, planting and harvesting alongside other workers in the field.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　acquisitive; production
engage
engage: standard unit headword
#383 TC TRIPLE
Ultimately, the young idealists (i) __________ the society they so fervently desired to reform: in their frustration, they reacted to the immediate situation by removing themselves from the postwar social and political arena, where the future of their country was being decided. In doing so, they (ii) __________ the salutary effects their rebellion should have (iii) __________.Ultimately, the young idealists (i) __________ the society they so fervently desired to reform: in their frustration, they reacted to the immediate situation by removing themselves from the postwar social and political arena, where the future of their country was being decided. In doing so, they (ii) __________ the salutary effects their rebellion should have (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　did a great disservice to; nullified; begotten
effect
effect: standard unit headword
#384 SE
Karin Knorr-Cetina argues that the production of technological knowledge is fundamentally __________: technological innovations arise within an "epistemic culture," that is, practitioners bonded by affinity who create and warrant emerging ideas.Karin Knorr-Cetina argues that the production of technological knowledge is fundamentally __________: technological innovations arise within an "epistemic culture," that is, practitioners bonded by affinity who create and warrant emerging ideas.
答案：B, F　collective; social
emerge
emerge: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　despair of; faithful

Some history books are pathbreaking. (i) __________ conventional wisdom, they inspire scholars to (ii) __________ lines of inquiry virtually unimagined before their publication. Other works, valuable in their own way, fill in gaps by challenging readers to rethink issues that had been thought to be (iii) __________.Some history books are pathbreaking. (i) __________ conventional wisdom, they inspire scholars to (ii) __________ lines of inquiry virtually unimagined before their publication. Other works, valuable in their own way, fill in gaps by challenging readers to rethink issues that had been thought to be (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　Revising; pursue; resolved
virtually

答案：B, F　a rigorous; a faithful

Blank 2

#389 TC TRIPLE
The poem is not nearly as (i) __________ as it has often been said to be. Granted, it would be (ii) __________ to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. But the poem is more self-contained than it appears when you read only fragments of it, and its allusions are often (iii) __________ by their contexts or by their repeated appearances in changing contexts.The poem is not nearly as (i) __________ as it has often been said to be. Granted, it would be (ii) __________ to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. But the poem is more self-contained than it appears when you read only fragments of it, and its allusions are often (iii) __________ by their contexts or by their repeated appearances in changing contexts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　difficult; absurd; explained

The athlete had long been recognized as having two sides to her personality: she was an imperious, steely competitor and at the same time __________ sportswoman.The athlete had long been recognized as having two sides to her personality: she was an imperious, steely competitor and at the same time __________ sportswoman.
答案：A, E　a magnanimous; a generous
imperious

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　jocose; exultant

#392 TC TRIPLE
Lebrecht recounts many entertaining scandals, (i) __________ himself on some points in deference to the laws of libel. His anecdotes seem (ii) __________ but when carefully scrutinized, their accuracy is less than impressive. What rings false is his picture of the impresarios as unerring Machiavellian schemers-villains who never (iii) __________ in their efforts to pile up a fortune and ruin the world of music. However, my experience in the concert and recording world suggests that there is more bumbling than sinister plotting.Lebrecht recounts many entertaining scandals, (i) __________ himself on some points in deference to the laws of libel. His anecdotes seem (ii) __________ but when carefully scrutinized, their accuracy is less than impressive. What rings false is his picture of the impresarios as unerring Machiavellian schemers-villains who never (iii) __________ in their efforts to pile up a fortune and ruin the world of music. However, my experience in the concert and recording world suggests that there is more bumbling than sinister plotting.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　restraining; plausible; blunder
anecdote
anecdote: standard unit headword

Though the autobiography generally reflects the author's distinctively benign attitude toward others' foibles, some of its observations have __________ quality, revealing an underlying disdain not evident in earlier works.Though the autobiography generally reflects the author's distinctively benign attitude toward others' foibles, some of its observations have __________ quality, revealing an underlying disdain not evident in earlier works.
答案：C, E　a derisive; a contemptuous

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　apparent; dependent on

It has been demonstrated that on Earth, no single landscape feature can be used as a quantitative indicator of past climatic conditions. The same is probably true for Mars; nevertheless, as the martian landscape is not as (i) __________ as the terrain on Earth, with no plate tectonics, no vegetation, less water, and lower erosion rates, the landscape's (ii) __________ the climate on Mars could be (iii) __________.It has been demonstrated that on Earth, no single landscape feature can be used as a quantitative indicator of past climatic conditions. The same is probably true for Mars; nevertheless, as the martian landscape is not as (i) __________ as the terrain on Earth, with no plate tectonics, no vegetation, less water, and lower erosion rates, the landscape's (ii) __________ the climate on Mars could be (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　complex; connection to; more direct

答案：B, E　contemplative; ruminative

For instance, he deplores the Royal Shakespeare Company’s eight-and-a-half hour version of Nicholas Nickleby, which many found (ii)__________.Mr. Billington, at times, can be a (i)__________. For instance, he deplores the Royal Shakespeare Company’s eight-and-a-half hour version of Nicholas Nickleby, which many found (ii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2

#398 TC TRIPLE
Reading Frankfurt's book, I worried that in the absence of specific examples or nuanced analysis, the unnamed postmodernists who are so (i) __________ in it might feel that they have been tarred by superficial and unsubstantiated generalizations. Of course, postmodernism is something of a (ii) __________ these days, and many readers may feel that no insult is too gross to heap on it. It arouses the same (iii) __________ that used to be aroused by idealism and skepticism.Reading Frankfurt's book, I worried that in the absence of specific examples or nuanced analysis, the unnamed postmodernists who are so (i) __________ in it might feel that they have been tarred by superficial and unsubstantiated generalizations. Of course, postmodernism is something of a (ii) __________ these days, and many readers may feel that no insult is too gross to heap on it. It arouses the same (iii) __________ that used to be aroused by idealism and skepticism.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　enthusiastically vilified; scapegoat; horror

The vivid, intelligent introduction to fundamental elements of classical music offered by conductor Michael Tilson Thomas in these programs has few precedents: Thomas is truly performing radical acts of __________.The vivid, intelligent introduction to fundamental elements of classical music offered by conductor Michael Tilson Thomas in these programs has few precedents: Thomas is truly performing radical acts of __________.
答案：A, E　elucidation; demystification

In years prior to the Civil War, Philadelphia’s African American press encouraged readers to be vaccinated against the smallpox. This journalistic campaign was initially (i)__________, appealing to readers’ sense of communal duty, and became even more (ii)__________ once the war started, as smallpox outbreaks began to occur on Philadelphia’s city skirts.In years prior to the Civil War, Philadelphia’s African American press encouraged readers to be vaccinated against the smallpox. This journalistic campaign was initially (i)__________, appealing to readers’ sense of communal duty, and became even more (ii)__________ once the war started, as smallpox outbreaks began to occur on Philadelphia’s city skirts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　hortatory; urgent

Blank 1
Blank 2

Common millet is easy to cultivate and appears to have been better suited to primitive agricultural practices than were other crops. Under (i) __________ environmental conditions, however, common millet cultivation may be (ii) __________ because of the millet's relatively (iii) __________ response to high soil fertility and abundant water availability.Neolithic people living in an arid northern region of China cultivated common millet more than did people living to their south. Common millet is easy to cultivate and appears to have been better suited to primitive agricultural practices than were other crops. Under (i) __________ environmental conditions, however, common millet cultivation may be (ii) __________ because of the millet's relatively (iii) __________ response to high soil fertility and abundant water availability.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　propitious; a disadvantageous choice; limited

答案：C, F　prudent; circumspect

At first willing to reconcile herself to the imposture, she is eventually impelled to rebel by her innate sense of justice and her formidable skills as a gumshoe.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　autonomous; a figurehead; conform to

Anna Barbauld's 1804 edition of Richardson's correspondence has been (i) __________. Although she (ii) __________ many of the letters that she included and made stylistic changes throughout, often for the worse, she was, by the standards of her time, quite (iii) __________ editor with regard to her respect for the original texts.Anna Barbauld's 1804 edition of Richardson's correspondence has been (i) __________. Although she (ii) __________ many of the letters that she included and made stylistic changes throughout, often for the worse, she was, by the standards of her time, quite (iii) __________ editor with regard to her respect for the original texts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　unjustly disparaged; abridged; a scrupulous

original: standard unit headword
#414 SE
Even if quantum logic has not been shown to be wrong, it has proved to be __________: it fails to link up in interesting ways with mainstream developments in mathematical physics.Even if quantum logic has not been shown to be wrong, it has proved to be __________: it fails to link up in interesting ways with mainstream developments in mathematical physics.
答案：C, D　sterile; unfruitful

interest: standard unit headword
#415 TC DOUBLE
The benefits offered by information technology do not (i) __________ the need for individual reasoning; for example, Internet users should not allow the reasoning process to be (ii) __________ the mere accumulation of raw data.The benefits offered by information technology do not (i) __________ the need for individual reasoning; for example, Internet users should not allow the reasoning process to be (ii) __________ the mere accumulation of raw data.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　diminish; supplanted by
precede
precede: standard unit headword
#416 TC TRIPLE
Thorough cleanings or restorations that try to return much-loved old master paintings to their original condition (i) __________. Quite apart from a reasonable wariness of paint loss, sheer loyalty to the (ii) __________ look of a work, dirt and all, prejudices many. There are also academics who (iii) __________ the removal of retouches and even of overpaintings, holding that even though they may date from years after the original composition, they embody history in themselves.Thorough cleanings or restorations that try to return much-loved old master paintings to their original condition (i) __________. Quite apart from a reasonable wariness of paint loss, sheer loyalty to the (ii) __________ look of a work, dirt and all, prejudices many. There are also academics who (iii) __________ the removal of retouches and even of overpaintings, holding that even though they may date from years after the original composition, they embody history in themselves.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　invariably face opposition; accustomed; militate against
original
original: standard unit headword
#417 SE
Although the author affects a breezy, ironic tone in much of his writing, his __________ gives way to palpable sadness when he writes about his experiences during the First World War.Although the author affects a breezy, ironic tone in much of his writing, his __________ gives way to palpable sadness when he writes about his experiences during the First World War.
答案：C, D　insouciance; detachment
ironic
ironic: standard unit headword
#418 TC DOUBLE
Scholarly works on detective stories often begin with (i) __________, suggesting that there is something vaguely wrong with adults who spend their time reading such fiction and certainly something (ii) __________ those who devote energy to its analysis.Scholarly works on detective stories often begin with (i) __________, suggesting that there is something vaguely wrong with adults who spend their time reading such fiction and certainly something (ii) __________ those who devote energy to its analysis.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　apologies; awry in
astute
astute: standard unit headword
#419 TC TRIPLE
Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　monolithic; supposed distinction; deleterious
effect
effect: standard unit headword
#420 SE
Edited collections of scholarly essays generally tend to be somewhat uneven: they suffer from the __________ subject matter of the various essays, the lack of an overarching and consistent thesis, and the variable quantity of the contributions.Edited collections of scholarly essays generally tend to be somewhat uneven: they suffer from the __________ subject matter of the various essays, the lack of an overarching and consistent thesis, and the variable quantity of the contributions.
答案：B, C　disparate; heterogeneous
engageengaging
engage: standard unit headword | engaging: standard unit headword
#421 TC DOUBLE
Although the political science professor's paper is quite (i) __________ about the government's problem, suggesting that they are part of (ii) __________ a process, the prognosis for the government is, on the contrary, actually quite auspicious.Although the political science professor's paper is quite (i) __________ about the government's problem, suggesting that they are part of (ii) __________ a process, the prognosis for the government is, on the contrary, actually quite auspicious.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　pessimistic; degenerative
circuitous
circuitous: standard unit headword
#422 TC TRIPLE
Because the term "memoir," unlike the term "autobiography," can suggest subjectivity rather than objectivity, Barton, in choosing to call his book a memoir, (i) __________ the accusation that his story may be (ii) __________. As Barton himself has noted, memoir promises only to give the reader an account of the author's memory, and no one but the author can therefore (iii) __________.Because the term "memoir," unlike the term "autobiography," can suggest subjectivity rather than objectivity, Barton, in choosing to call his book a memoir, (i) __________ the accusation that his story may be (ii) __________. As Barton himself has noted, memoir promises only to give the reader an account of the author's memory, and no one but the author can therefore (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　deflects; untrue; gauge its veracity
memoir
memoir: standard unit headword
#423 SE
Even the most hard-nosed critics could hardly deny that the novelist’s best books are __________: they are filled with energy, imagination, and something close to a white-hot inspiration.Even the most hard-nosed critics could hardly deny that the novelist’s best books are __________: they are filled with energy, imagination, and something close to a white-hot inspiration.
答案：C, E　spirited; vigorous
engageengaging
engage: standard unit headword | engaging: standard unit headword
#424 TC DOUBLE
There are far too many (i) __________ in the report, such as incorrect data (albeit on (ii) __________ points), inconsistency between the text and related tables, and discrepancies between the citations and the references.There are far too many (i) __________ in the report, such as incorrect data (albeit on (ii) __________ points), inconsistency between the text and related tables, and discrepancies between the citations and the references.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　little errors; minor
perplex
perplex: standard unit headword
#425 TC TRIPLE
It has been argued that the extinction of megamammals, such as mammoths, had a (i) __________ effect on certain species because megamammals were "keystone herbivore species": their disappearance (ii) __________ natural processes such as tree regeneration and shrub invasions of grassy glades, (iii) __________ nonmigratory grazers that feed on growing grass and pasturage.It has been argued that the extinction of megamammals, such as mammoths, had a (i) __________ effect on certain species because megamammals were "keystone herbivore species": their disappearance (ii) __________ natural processes such as tree regeneration and shrub invasions of grassy glades, (iii) __________ nonmigratory grazers that feed on growing grass and pasturage.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　negative; stimulated; disadvantaging
effect
effect: standard unit headword
#426 SE
Although its gray text blocks and black-and-white illustrations give it a sober mien, this one-stop resource can take the place of a dozen less __________ texts.Although its gray text blocks and black-and-white illustrations give it a sober mien, this one-stop resource can take the place of a dozen less __________ texts.
答案：A, F　exhaustive; comprehensive
originalinterest
original: standard unit headword | interest: standard unit headword
#427 TC DOUBLE
In the years prior to the Civil War, Philadelphia's African American press encouraged readers to be vaccinated against smallpox. This journalistic campaign was initially (i) __________, appealing to readers' sense of communal duty, and became even more (ii) __________ once the war started, as smallpox outbreaks began to occur on Philadelphia's outskirts.In the years prior to the Civil War, Philadelphia's African American press encouraged readers to be vaccinated against smallpox. This journalistic campaign was initially (i) __________, appealing to readers' sense of communal duty, and became even more (ii) __________ once the war started, as smallpox outbreaks began to occur on Philadelphia's outskirts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　hortatory; urgent
ineffective
ineffective: standard unit headword
#428 TC TRIPLE
In framing old paintings, after the gilding comes a process that is freer and more like art: toning the gold and distressing it, so that the frame does not look new. Since verisimilitude is sought, the aim in adding apparent age to the new surface must be to (i) __________, circumventing the human eye's adeptness at spotting anything that seems (ii) __________. Different framers have different ways of (iii) __________ the effect of chance.In framing old paintings, after the gilding comes a process that is freer and more like art: toning the gold and distressing it, so that the frame does not look new. Since verisimilitude is sought, the aim in adding apparent age to the new surface must be to (i) __________, circumventing the human eye's adeptness at spotting anything that seems (ii) __________. Different framers have different ways of (iii) __________ the effect of chance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　conceal the artifice; intentional; mimicking
effect
effect: standard unit headword
#429 SE
The author's comparisons of Sontag's work to that of Walter Benjamin are __________: while the former is admittedly an influential figure, the latter is undoubtedly one of the most important critics of his era.The author's comparisons of Sontag's work to that of Walter Benjamin are __________: while the former is admittedly an influential figure, the latter is undoubtedly one of the most important critics of his era.
答案：A, B　overblown; inflated

#430 TC DOUBLE
It was fine to be (i) __________ in the old days when papers were still not losing readers and ad revenue was not tough to come by, but many editors today are uneasy about bludgeoning their readers with the inherently (ii) __________ work of cartoonists.It was fine to be (i) __________ in the old days when papers were still not losing readers and ad revenue was not tough to come by, but many editors today are uneasy about bludgeoning their readers with the inherently (ii) __________ work of cartoonists.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　opinionated; unbalanced
flaw
flaw: standard unit headword
#431 TC TRIPLE
There can be little question that by the fourteenth century the capacity to (i) __________ a doubtless restricted written Latin was available in virtually all English communities. It would, of course, be (ii) __________ to suppose that all the wealth of material available in Latin since 1100 was put to service in the cultural formation of every writer of business Latin in provincial England, but even schoolbooks (iii) __________ some elements of classical education. Latin literacy was not, in these circumstances, exclusive.There can be little question that by the fourteenth century the capacity to (i) __________ a doubtless restricted written Latin was available in virtually all English communities. It would, of course, be (ii) __________ to suppose that all the wealth of material available in Latin since 1100 was put to service in the cultural formation of every writer of business Latin in provincial England, but even schoolbooks (iii) __________ some elements of classical education. Latin literacy was not, in these circumstances, exclusive.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　deploy; fanciful; purveyed
virtually
virtually: standard unit headword
#432 SE
In spite of its outwardly __________ demeanor, Guatemala City was a compelling place to be in 1954.In spite of its outwardly __________ demeanor, Guatemala City was a compelling place to be in 1954.
答案：E, F　workaday; quotidian
engageengaging
engage: standard unit headword | engaging: standard unit headword
#433 TC DOUBLE
To the avid reader of E.O. Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i) __________, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. Nonetheless, new thoughts have been mixed in with the old to produce a book remarkable for its (ii) __________ and ambition.To the avid reader of E.O. Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i) __________, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. Nonetheless, new thoughts have been mixed in with the old to produce a book remarkable for its (ii) __________ and ambition.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　predictable; purview
startling
startling: standard unit headword
#434 SE
Scientists should hope the faults in their theories will be __________ their peers since the refutation of one hypothesis can free its originator to develop a better one.Scientists should hope the faults in their theories will be __________ their peers since the refutation of one hypothesis can free its originator to develop a better one.
答案：A, C　discerned by; discovered by

opaque: standard unit headword
#435 TC TRIPLE
Within the culture as a whole, natural science has been so successful that the word scientific is used in (i)__________ manner; it is often assumed that to call something “scientific” is to imply that its reliability has been (ii)__________ by research whose results cannot reasonably be (iii)__________.Within the culture as a whole, natural science has been so successful that the word scientific is used in (i)__________ manner; it is often assumed that to call something “scientific” is to imply that its reliability has been (ii)__________ by research whose results cannot reasonably be (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　an honorific; established; disputed
ironic
ironic: standard unit headword
#436 TC DOUBLE
Although the university president can deploy considerable (i) __________, and her emotional reticence never seems rude, one nevertheless cannot help being aware of the (ii) __________ behind the affable public persona.Although the university president can deploy considerable (i) __________, and her emotional reticence never seems rude, one nevertheless cannot help being aware of the (ii) __________ behind the affable public persona.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　charm; reserve
cunning
cunning: standard unit headword
#437 SE
Doris Kearns Goodwin’s elegant, incisive study of Lincoln __________ those whose knowledge of Lincoln is an amalgam of high school history and popular mythology as well as those who are experts.Doris Kearns Goodwin’s elegant, incisive study of Lincoln __________ those whose knowledge of Lincoln is an amalgam of high school history and popular mythology as well as those who are experts.
答案：C, D　gratify; please

perplex: standard unit headword
#438 TC TRIPLE
The skin of the poison dart frog contains deadly poison called batrachotoxins. But the (i)__________ of the toxins has remained an enigma, as the frog does not (ii)__________ them. Now an analysis suggests that the melyrid beetle is the source. Collected beetle specimens all contained batrachotoxins, suggesting that these beetles are (iii)__________ by the frogs.The skin of the poison dart frog contains deadly poison called batrachotoxins. But the (i)__________ of the toxins has remained an enigma, as the frog does not (ii)__________ them. Now an analysis suggests that the melyrid beetle is the source. Collected beetle specimens all contained batrachotoxins, suggesting that these beetles are (iii)__________ by the frogs.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　origin; produce; eaten
effect
effect: standard unit headword
#439 TC DOUBLE
In aging bees, cognitive decline is (i) __________ under certain social conditions--for example, when the colony divides by swarming or when foragers or young nurse bees are removed from the colony. Each of these circumstances causes older bees to become cognitively (ii) __________, while juvenile bees, by contrast, exhibit a faster age-related cognitive decline.In aging bees, cognitive decline is (i) __________ under certain social conditions--for example, when the colony divides by swarming or when foragers or young nurse bees are removed from the colony. Each of these circumstances causes older bees to become cognitively (ii) __________, while juvenile bees, by contrast, exhibit a faster age-related cognitive decline.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　reversed; younger
sluggish
sluggish: standard unit headword
#440 SE
Ancient cave painters explored every surface, and although they bypassed certain walls that to us seem just as suitable for decoration as ones they chose, the placement of the art apparently wasn’t __________.Ancient cave painters explored every surface, and although they bypassed certain walls that to us seem just as suitable for decoration as ones they chose, the placement of the art apparently wasn’t __________.
答案：B, F　capricious; impulsive
strenuous
strenuous: standard unit headword
#441 TC TRIPLE
Anna Gavalda’s fiction is appealing largely because of her writing styles, which is certainly (i)__________. It is heavy on dialogue (and light on embellishment), features very short sections (of often only a few sentence) in rapid succession, and in general favors an unadorned directness. Not surprisingly, this style works better in short-story form than in novels, and the story collection I Wish Someone Were Waiting for me Somewhere (ii)__________ her attempt to (iii)__________ it in a novel, as in, for example, Someone I Loved.Anna Gavalda’s fiction is appealing largely because of her writing styles, which is certainly (i)__________. It is heavy on dialogue (and light on embellishment), features very short sections (of often only a few sentence) in rapid succession, and in general favors an unadorned directness. Not surprisingly, this style works better in short-story form than in novels, and the story collection I Wish Someone Were Waiting for me Somewhere (ii)__________ her attempt to (iii)__________ it in a novel, as in, for example, Someone I Loved.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　distinctive; impresses considerably more than; sustain
precede
precede: standard unit headword
#442 TC DOUBLE
Especially when poorly organized, alliances among nations can send messages that (i) __________ either credible threats or credible assurances, thus making stable coercive diplomacy with enemies (short of war) (ii) __________ .Especially when poorly organized, alliances among nations can send messages that (i) __________ either credible threats or credible assurances, thus making stable coercive diplomacy with enemies (short of war) (ii) __________ .
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　undercut; harder to maintain
proliferate
proliferate: standard unit headword
#443 SE
Although people often describe the correct trajectory for a thrown or moving object, their efforts to explain that trajectory in terms of physics can reveal __________ understanding of the forces acting on the object.Although people often describe the correct trajectory for a thrown or moving object, their efforts to explain that trajectory in terms of physics can reveal __________ understanding of the forces acting on the object.
答案：A, D　a naïve; an unsophisticated
nuance
nuance: standard unit headword
#444 TC TRIPLE
The usual (i)__________ spending public monies on scientific projects is that such projects have the potential to make our lives healthier, safer, and more productive. However, the fact that science—even "pure" science—can strengthen democracy and promote public participation in the political process is hardly ever (ii)__________. It should be that scientific literacy (iii)__________ democracy, and this is an important ancillary benefit of the promotion of science.The usual (i)__________ spending public monies on scientific projects is that such projects have the potential to make our lives healthier, safer, and more productive. However, the fact that science—even "pure" science—can strengthen democracy and promote public participation in the political process is hardly ever (ii)__________. It should be that scientific literacy (iii)__________ democracy, and this is an important ancillary benefit of the promotion of science.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　rationale for; mentioned; energizes
precedent
precedent: standard unit headword

The author argues that (i) __________ of economic growth data for the African continent as a whole are often (ii) __________ because they obscure the diversity of growth rates among individual countries.The author argues that (i) __________ of economic growth data for the African continent as a whole are often (ii) __________ because they obscure the diversity of growth rates among individual countries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　aggregations; misleading

答案：B, E　a coherent; an orderly

Collected beetle specimens all contained batrachotoxins, suggesting that these beetles are (iii) __________ by the frogs.The skin of the poison dart frog contains deadly poisons called batrachotoxins. But the (i) __________ of the toxins has remained an enigma, as the frog does not (ii) __________ them. Now an analysis suggests that the melyrid beetle is the source. Collected beetle specimens all contained batrachotoxins, suggesting that these beetles are (iii) __________ by the frogs.

Blank 1
Blank 2

As a relative proposition, however, her position was more (ii) __________: compared to other groups in New Guinea, the Mountain Arapesh might have seemed unaggressive and unwarlike.Mead was certainly (i) __________ to state the case for Arapesh nonviolence and gentleness in absolute terms, for the Mountain Arapesh had an established tradition of warfare. As a relative proposition, however, her position was more (ii) __________: compared to other groups in New Guinea, the Mountain Arapesh might have seemed unaggressive and unwarlike.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　mistaken; defensible

Nevertheless, that does not mean male care is (ii) __________ situations in which females mate with more than one male; studies of savanna baboons show that male care certainly can occur in nonmonogamous systems.There is little question that in primates there is (i) __________ between monogamous mating partnerships and extensive male care of infants. Nevertheless, that does not mean male care is (ii) __________ situations in which females mate with more than one male; studies of savanna baboons show that male care certainly can occur in nonmonogamous systems.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　an association; precluded by

答案：A, C　contingent; circumstantial

Of course, it is human nature to regard the world that we are used to as (ii)__________. The oceans, prairies, and mountain chains - even the air we breathe - seem the norm and therefore (iii)__________.Compared to Earth over most of its 4 to 6-billion-year history, the world we live in today is quite (i)__________. Of course, it is human nature to regard the world that we are used to as (ii)__________. The oceans, prairies, and mountain chains - even the air we breathe - seem the norm and therefore (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　atypical; permanent; eternal
